  Note: 'Long-term framework agreements, master service agreements (MSA), or term contracts established with repeat clients, defining pre-agreed rates, scope categories, KPIs, and call-off procedures for multiple projects over a defined period. Captures agreement title, client account, agreement type (framework, MSA, term contract, blanket order), effective date, expiry date, total ceiling value, call-off mechanism, performance KPIs, renewal terms, and extension options. Common with public-sector clients (e.g., highways agencies, utilities) who procure repeat works under a single competitive framework.'
}

Table client.jv_participant {
  jv_participant_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the jv_participant association']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the participating client account entity']
  jv_structure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the Joint Venture structure in which the account participates']
  committed_capital_amount decimal [note: 'The total capital amount this account has committed to contribute to the JV, as defined in the JV agreement. Specific to each participant\'s obligation within the JV.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the committed_capital_amount. Required because JV participants may commit capital in different currencies in cross-border JV structures.']
  equity_percentage decimal [note: 'The percentage equity stake held by this account in the JV structure. Belongs to the participation record, not to the account or JV structure alone, as each participant holds a distinct share.']
  exit_date date [note: 'The date on which this account exited or is contractually scheduled to exit the JV. Null if the participant remains active through JV dissolution.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this account is currently an active participant in the JV. Set to false when exit_date is reached or participation is formally terminated.']
  join_date date [note: 'The date on which this account formally joined the JV as a participating entity, as executed in the JV agreement or a subsequent accession deed.']
  participation_role varchar [note: 'The governance and operational role this account holds within the JV structure. Determines voting rights, management responsibilities, and liability exposure.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Participation role between account and jv_structure. It captures each client entity\'s formal membership in a Joint Venture arrangement, including their equity stake, committed capital, governance role, and tenure dates. Each record links one account to one jv_structure with attributes that exist only in the context of that specific participation — data that cannot reside on either the account or the JV structure alone.. Existence Justification: In construction, Joint Ventures are co-ownership vehicles where multiple client entities (accounts) simultaneously participate as equity partners in a single JV structure. A single account (e.g., a large infrastructure developer) can participate in multiple JV structures across different projects, and a single JV structure by definition has multiple participating accounts. The current model only captures the lead sponsor via primary_jv_account_id on jv_structure, leaving all non-lead participants entirely unmodelled — a fundamental gap in the business model.'
}

Table contract.agreement {
  agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the master contract record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the client (owner) of the contract.']
  client_opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.client_opportunity. Business justification: Pipeline-to-revenue conversion reporting requires tracing which CRM opportunity resulted in which awarded contract. BD and commercial teams track win rates, opportunity-to-contract conversion, and rev']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Contracts are executed under a specific legal entity (company code) in multi-entity construction groups and JVs. This determines the contracting legal entity for financial reporting, tax jurisdiction,']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project that the contract supports.']
  env_impact_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.env_impact_assessment. Business justification: Environmental Impact Assessment linkage: contracts need to attach the approved assessment to satisfy regulatory and client environmental requirements.']
  framework_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.client_framework_agreement. Business justification: Framework agreement ceiling value tracking and call-off compliance require each contract (agreement) to reference its governing client framework agreement. Construction contract managers must verify t']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Required for Permit Compliance Tracking: contracts must reference the specific permit authorizing the work; permits are reviewed before contract award.']
  rfp_issuance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.rfp_issuance. Business justification: Procurement compliance and contract setup validation require tracing which RFP issuance (with its defined LD rates, bond %, delivery model, and evaluation criteria) gave rise to each contract. Auditor']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary contractor responsible for delivering the work.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the agreement.']
  amendment_effective_date date [note: 'Date the amendment becomes effective.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Sequential identifier for a contract amendment.']
  amendment_type varchar [note: 'Type of amendment (e.g., supplemental, variation, novation).']
  award_date date [note: 'Date the contract was formally awarded to the contractor.']
  boq_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the Bill of Quantities associated with the contract.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Regulatory and statutory compliance obligations attached to the agreement.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'External contract identifier used in all business communications and legal documents.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the agreement (e.g., EPC, GMP, Lump Sum, Unit Rate, Design-Build, Design-Bid-Build, PPP, BOT). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EPC|GMP|LumpSum|UnitRate|DesignBuild|DesignBidBuild|PPP|BOT — promote to reference product]']
  contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original monetary value of the agreement as agreed at award.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contract record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the contract value.']
  defects_liability_period_months varchar [note: 'Duration after completion during which the contractor is liable for defects.']
  dispute_resolution varchar [note: 'Method for resolving disputes (e.g., arbitration, litigation, mediation).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date the agreement terminates or expires (null for open‑ended contracts).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date the agreement becomes legally binding.']
  environmental_impact_assessment_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to the approved environmental impact assessment linked to the contract.']
  extension_of_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days granted as an extension of time (EOT) under the contract.']
  geographic_location varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code where the contract work is performed.']
  governing_law varchar [note: 'Jurisdiction whose law governs the contract.']
  health_safety_plan_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to the HSE plan governing safety on the contract site.']
  insurance_requirements varchar [note: 'Required insurance coverage and limits for the contract.']
  liquidated_damages_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount payable for each day of delay beyond the agreed completion date.']
  milestone_schedule varchar [note: 'Key project milestones linked to contract payments.']
  notice_to_proceed_date date [note: 'Date the client issued the Notice to Proceed (NTP) to start work.']
  payment_schedule varchar [note: 'Description of payment milestones, dates, and conditions.']
  performance_bond_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Security amount posted by the contractor to guarantee performance.']
  retention_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of each payment retained until contract close‑out.']
  revised_completion_date date [note: 'New contract completion date after amendment.']
  revised_contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Contract value after applying the amendment.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the work scope covered by the agreement.']
  special_conditions varchar [note: 'Any special or bespoke conditions that modify standard contract terms.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the contract record.']
  wbs_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier linking the contract to the associated Work Breakdown Structure.']

  Note: 'Master contract record representing the authoritative SSOT for all contractual agreements in the construction domain. Captures FIDIC-based EPC, GMP (Guaranteed Maximum Price), lump-sum, unit-rate, DB (Design-Build), DBB (Design-Bid-Build), PPP (Public-Private Partnership), and BOT (Build-Operate-Transfer) contract types via a type classification attribute. Stores contract identity, scope narrative, detailed scope of works including WBS (Work Breakdown Structure) reference, deliverable descriptions, technical specifications reference, BOQ (Bill of Quantities) linkage, geographic boundaries, and exclusions. Records execution dates, NTP (Notice to Proceed) date, contract value, currency, governing law, dispute resolution mechanism, contract status lifecycle from award through close-out, and particular/special conditions that modify standard form terms. Maintains status transition history for audit and lifecycle analytics. Records all formal amendments including supplemental agreements, deed of variations, and novation agreements with amendment number, type, effective date, revised contract value, revised completion date, and execution status. Serves as the authoritative scope baseline against which change orders and EOT claims are evaluated.'
}

Table contract.party {
  party_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each party associated with a contract.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.account. Business justification: Required for Contract Administration Report linking each contract party to the client account it represents, enabling account‑level performance and compliance tracking.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Parties are associated with the agreement they belong to; linking provides inbound/outbound relationship.']
  client_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.contact. Business justification: Needed for Communication Log and Regulatory Contact Register to identify the primary client contact responsible for a contract party.']
  jv_structure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.jv_structure. Business justification: JV contract administration requires linking each JV party record to the governing JV structure to access profit-sharing basis, liability structure, and management arrangements. Construction contract m']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Maps each contract party that is a supplier to its vendor record, enabling vendor performance tracking per contract.']
  vendor_qualification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_qualification. Business justification: A contract party that is a vendor must have been qualified before contract award. Linking party to vendor_qualification enables prequalification compliance verification, insurance and bonding capacity']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the party.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Additional address information (suite, floor, etc.).']
  bank_account_number varchar [note: 'Account number used for contract‑related payments.']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'Financial institution where the party\'s account is held.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the mailing address.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Main email used for contractual communications.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for reaching the party.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code for the mailing address.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the party record was first entered into the system.']
  insurance_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the insurance policy covering the party.']
  insurance_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the provided insurance coverage ends.']
  is_jv_partner boolean [note: 'True when the party participates as a JV partner in the contract.']
  is_primary_party boolean [note: 'True when the party is the main responsible entity for the contract.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code where the party is legally registered.']
  legal_entity_name varchar [note: 'Official registered name of the corporate entity.']
  liability_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary cap on the party\'s contractual liability.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments, observations, or special conditions related to the party.']
  participation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of ownership or profit share for joint‑venture partners.']
  party_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the party (individual or organization) as it appears in the contract.']
  party_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the party within the contract management system.']
  party_type varchar [note: 'Category that defines the business role of the party in construction contracts.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Agreed payment schedule (e.g., Net 30).']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the mailing address.']
  record_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record that originated the party data.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Government‑issued registration identifier for the legal entity.']
  role_in_contract varchar [note: 'The functional role the party plays for the specific contract (e.g., owner, contractor).']
  signatory_authority boolean [note: 'True when the party is authorized to execute the contract on behalf of its organization.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province component of the mailing address.']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Government‑issued tax ID for the party.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the latest modification to the party record.']

  Note: 'Association entity capturing all parties bound to a contract agreement, including the employer/client, general contractor (GC), joint venture (JV) partners, engineer/consultant, and guarantors. Records party role, signatory authority, legal entity name, registration number, jurisdiction, and participation percentage for JV arrangements. Enables multi-party contract structures common in EPC and PPP delivery models.'
}

Table contract.scope {
  scope_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the contract scope record.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Each contract scope belongs to a single agreement; adding agreement_id creates a parent link and eliminates the silo.']
  boq_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the BOQ that quantifies the scope items.']
  cost_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_code. Business justification: Contract scope items are mapped to finance cost codes for job costing and budget allocation. This mapping is fundamental to construction cost control — each scope element must be coded to enable budge']
  project_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.site. Business justification: Contract scope defines work performed at a specific site. Linking contract_scope to site enables site-based contract reporting, HSE compliance tracking per site, and eliminates denormalized site_addre']
  technical_specification_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the technical specification linked to the scope.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the WBS element that the scope aligns to.']
  wbs_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to schedule.wbs_node. Business justification: Contract scope items must be mapped to schedule WBS nodes for earned value measurement and payment certification. In construction, the contract BOQ/scope is aligned to the programme WBS to enable cost']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Actual date work on the scope was completed.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual date work on the scope began.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the scope was formally approved.']
  attachments_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether supporting documents are attached to the scope record.']
  change_order_count varchar [note: 'Number of change orders that have been applied to this scope.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the scope work is performed.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Text describing regulatory or compliance conditions tied to the scope.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code where the scope is located.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the scope record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  dlp_end_date date [note: 'Date when the defects liability period expires.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the scope ceases to be binding (nullable for open‑ended scopes).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the scope becomes contractually binding.']
  eot_entitlement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the scope includes entitlement to extensions of time.']
  exclusions varchar [note: 'Text describing work items explicitly excluded from the scope.']
  geographic_area varchar [note: 'Textual description of the geographic boundaries covered by the scope.']
  inclusions varchar [note: 'Text describing work items explicitly included in the scope.']
  liquidated_damages_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount stipulated for liquidated damages under the contract.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the scope.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Planned completion date for the scope.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Planned start date for execution of the scope.']
  quantity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the total quantity (e.g., cubic meters, kilograms).']
  region varchar [note: 'Region or state within the country for the scope location.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision number for the scope document.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk classification for the scope.']
  scope_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the scope of works.']
  scope_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or title of the scope of works.']
  scope_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the scope.']
  scope_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the scope (e.g., design, construction, procurement).']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the scope before taxes and adjustments.']
  total_quantity decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate quantity of all items covered by the scope.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the scope record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier (e.g., v1.0, v2.1) for the scope.']
  wbs_reference varchar [note: 'Human‑readable WBS code or description.']

  Note: 'Defines the detailed scope of works attached to a contract agreement, including WBS (Work Breakdown Structure) reference, deliverable descriptions, technical specifications reference, BOQ (Bill of Quantities) linkage, geographic boundaries, and exclusions. Serves as the authoritative scope baseline against which change orders and EOT (Extension of Time) claims are evaluated.'
}

Table contract.contract_milestone {
  contract_milestone_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the contract milestone record.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent contract to which this milestone belongs.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project associated with the contract milestone.']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_permit. Business justification: Construction milestones (e.g., \'commence piling\', \'open to traffic\') are gated by specific permits. Project controls teams verify permit validity before approving milestone completion. This link suppo']
  project_milestone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_milestone. Business justification: Contract milestones (payment triggers, LD triggers) must be reconciled against project schedule milestones in Primavera. Direct FK enables contract administrators to verify contractual milestone dates']
  responsible_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the party (e.g., contractor, project manager) responsible for delivering the milestone.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.site. Business justification: Sectional and practical completion milestones are tied to specific physical sites. Milestone certificate issuance, LD triggering, and DLP tracking all require site-level milestone reporting. Construct']
  work_front_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.work_front. Business justification: Milestones are tied to work fronts for progress monitoring and payment certification.']
  actual_date date [note: 'Date the milestone was actually achieved or certified.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of any amendment made to the milestone.']
  amendment_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for the most recent amendment to the milestone.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates compliance of the milestone with contractual and regulatory requirements.']
  contract_milestone_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing additional context for the milestone.']
  contract_milestone_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the milestone.']
  cost_variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between milestone_value and amount actually paid.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the milestone record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the milestone value.']
  defects_notified_date date [note: 'Date the employer formally notified the contractor of defects.']
  dlp_end_date date [note: 'End date of the Defects Liability Period.']
  dlp_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the Defects Liability Period associated with the milestone.']
  final_certificate_issued_date date [note: 'Date the final completion certificate was issued.']
  forecast_date date [note: 'Updated forecasted date based on progress and risk assessments.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the milestone is deemed critical for project schedule or payment.']
  ld_rate_per_day decimal(10,2) [note: 'Daily rate applied if liquidated damages are triggered.']
  ld_triggered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether liquidated damages have been incurred for this milestone.']
  liquidated_damages_applicable boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether liquidated damages may be assessed for this milestone.']
  milestone_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the milestone, used in contracts and payment schedules.']
  milestone_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the milestone (e.g., Notice to Proceed, Practical Completion).']
  milestone_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the milestone type according to contract terminology.']
  milestone_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the milestone, often linked to a payment certificate.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments entered by users.']
  outstanding_defects_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any defects remain unresolved at DLP expiry.']
  payment_certificate_date date [note: 'Date the payment certificate was issued.']
  payment_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the payment certificate linked to the milestone.']
  performance_certificate_date date [note: 'Date the performance certificate was issued.']
  performance_certificate_issued boolean [note: 'Flag showing if a performance certificate has been issued for the milestone.']
  planned_date date [note: 'Date originally scheduled for the milestone in the contract baseline.']
  rectification_deadline date [note: 'Deadline by which the contractor must rectify notified defects.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to specific regulatory clause or standard governing the milestone (e.g., FIDIC Clause 12.1).']
  retention_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the milestone value retained until final acceptance.']
  retention_release_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for releasing retained amounts after final acceptance.']
  schedule_variance_days varchar [note: 'Difference in days between planned_date and actual_date.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the milestone record.']

  Note: 'Tracks contractually obligated milestones, key dates, and DLP (Defects Liability Period) obligations within a contract. Covers NTP (Notice to Proceed), sectional completion dates, practical completion, DLP start/end dates, performance certificate issuance, and final certificate dates. Records planned date, forecast date, actual achieved date, milestone value (if linked to payment), milestone type, and milestone status. Manages the complete DLP register including defects notified by the employer, contractor rectification commitments, rectification deadlines, outstanding defects at DLP expiry, and the transition from practical completion through to final certificate issuance and retention release. Used for LD (Liquidated Damages) trigger assessment, payment certification, DLP management, and retention release scheduling.'
}

Table contract.payment_schedule {
  payment_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the payment schedule.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the contract to which this payment schedule belongs.']
  contract_milestone_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the milestone associated with the payment.']
  phase_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.phase. Business justification: In phased construction contracts, payment installments are structured around project phase completions (e.g., 30% on completion of foundations phase). Linking payment_schedule to phase enables phase-b']
  actual_payment_date date [note: 'Date the payment was actually made.']
  advance_balance_remaining decimal(18,2) [note: 'Outstanding advance amount yet to be recovered.']
  advance_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of the advance payment disbursed.']
  advance_payment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this schedule includes an advance payment component.']
  advance_recovery_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative amount recovered from subsequent payments.']
  business_event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp representing the business event that generated the schedule, e.g., milestone approval.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment schedule record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the payment amounts.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any discount applied to the gross amount before tax.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which the payment is contractually due.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount before taxes, discounts, or retentions.']
  guarantee_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the advance payment guarantee document.']
  installment_number varchar [note: 'Sequence number of this payment within the overall schedule.']
  is_final_payment boolean [note: 'True if this schedule represents the final payment of the contract.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle state of the payment schedule.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount payable after tax, discount, and retention adjustments.']
  net_amount_after_discount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net payable amount after discount and before tax.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks or observations related to the payment schedule.']
  payment_certificate_date date [note: 'Date the payment certificate was issued.']
  payment_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the payment certificate issued for this schedule.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Means by which the payment will be transferred.']
  payment_method_reference varchar [note: 'Reference details for the payment method, such as bank account number.']
  payment_schedule_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context for the payment schedule.']
  payment_source varchar [note: 'Originating party responsible for the payment.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the payment.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Contractual terms governing payment timing, e.g., Net 30.']
  payment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payment schedule based on its nature.']
  retention_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount retained from the payment as per contract terms.']
  retention_release_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of retention to be released on the scheduled release date.']
  retention_release_date date [note: 'Date when the retained amount is scheduled to be released.']
  schedule_number varchar [note: 'External reference number for the payment schedule as defined in the contract.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'Date the payment is scheduled to be processed.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component applied to the gross amount.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate percentage for the payment.']
  total_installments varchar [note: 'Total number of installments defined in the contract.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the payment schedule record.']

  Note: 'Defines the contractual payment schedule governing when and how much the employer is obligated to pay the contractor, including full advance payment (mobilization payment) lifecycle management. Captures payment terms, payment intervals, payment due dates, payment method, currency, and supports both milestone-based and time-based payment structures as defined under FIDIC and GMP contract forms. Manages the complete advance payment lifecycle: advance payment amount, percentage of contract value, disbursement date, repayment/recovery schedule, cumulative amount recovered through payment certificate deductions, outstanding advance balance, advance payment guarantee reference, and advance payment status from initial disbursement through full recovery. Serves as the authoritative SSOT for all payment scheduling and advance payment tracking, alongside progress payment and retention release scheduling.'
}

Table contract.payment_certificate {
  payment_certificate_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the payment certificate record.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master contract to which this certificate belongs.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the project associated with the contract.']
  contract_milestone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_milestone. Business justification: payment_certificate currently stores milestone_code as a denormalized STRING. In construction, payment certificates are frequently triggered by milestone achievement (e.g., IPC issued upon reaching a ']
  payment_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.payment_schedule. Business justification: A payment certificate (IPC/FPC) is issued to fulfill a specific scheduled payment installment defined in the payment_schedule. Linking payment_certificate to payment_schedule enables reconciliation be']
  progress_measurement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.progress_measurement. Business justification: Payment certification in construction is directly based on measured progress. Engineers certify payment against a specific progress measurement record. This link enables audit traceability from paymen']
  progress_update_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to schedule.progress_update. Business justification: Payment certificates are issued based on a specific progress update period. In construction, the certifying engineer references the schedule progress update (SPI, percent complete, earned value) when ']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.site. Business justification: Payment certificates are issued per site/section in multi-site contracts. Finance and commercial teams filter payment certificates by site for cash flow reporting, site-level cost control, and client ']
  trade_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to bid.trade_package. Business justification: Payment certificates in construction are issued per trade package to track certified value against awarded package budget. Project controls teams run \'certified vs awarded by trade package\' reports fo']
  actual_payment_date date [note: 'Date on which the payment was actually received.']
  advance_recovery_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount recovered against any previously paid advances.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the certificate was approved.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to each payment certificate, following the convention CP########.']
  certification_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the certificate was formally issued.']
  certification_version varchar [note: 'Version number for revised certificates.']
  certified_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Gross amount certified for payment before deductions.']
  change_order_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of any change order affecting this payment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the certificate record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO code of the currency for the payment.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'Date the invoice was issued.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the invoice generated for this certificate.']
  is_advance_recovered boolean [note: 'True when an advance payment is being recovered.']
  is_ld_applied boolean [note: 'True when liquidated damages are deducted.']
  is_pay_when_paid boolean [note: 'Indicates whether payment is contingent on receipt of funds from the client.']
  is_retention_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates if retention is being held for this certificate.']
  ld_deduction_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Deduction applied for liquidated damages, if any.']
  net_amount_due decimal(15,2) [note: 'Final payable amount after all deductions.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations related to the certificate.']
  payment_certificate_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the certificate.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the net amount is contractually due.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Means by which the payment will be made.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the payment transaction.']
  payment_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the certificate is for an interim or final payment.']
  retention_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount retained per contract terms, deducted from the certified amount.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Tax component applied to the certified amount.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the tax regime applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the certificate record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each interim payment certificate (IPC) and final payment certificate (FPC) issued under a main contract or subcontract. Captures certified amount, retention deducted, advance payment recovery, LD deductions, net amount due, certification date, certifier identity, payment due date, and actual payment date. Supports both main contract certification (FIDIC Clause 14) and subcontract payment certification via a contract-level discriminator distinguishing main contract from subcontract certificates. Records subcontract payment applications, certified amounts, and enables back-to-back payment flow management between main contract receipts and subcontractor disbursements. Supports cash flow forecasting, pay-when-paid clause administration, and unified payment reconciliation across the full contract hierarchy.'
}

Table contract.contract_change_order {
  contract_change_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'System generated unique identifier for the contract change order record.']
  affected_contract_milestone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_milestone. Business justification: A change order frequently impacts a specific contractual milestone — most commonly the completion milestone (revised_completion_date is already on the CCO). Adding affected_contract_milestone_id links']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original contract to which this change order applies.']
  estimate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to bid.estimate. Business justification: Change orders are priced using a revised estimate produced by the estimating team. The change order must reference that estimate for cost substantiation, variation audit trails, and contract value rec']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_permit. Business justification: Change orders altering scope, location, or construction method require a new or amended permit before execution. Contract administrators track which permit amendment is triggered by each change order ']
  scope_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_scope. Business justification: A contract change order (CO/variation) modifies a specific scope of work defined in contract_scope. contract_change_order has scope_description (a change-specific description of what is being changed)']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the contractor or subcontractor responsible for executing the change order work.']
  work_front_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.work_front. Business justification: Change orders frequently affect a particular work front; the link supports impact analysis per front.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of adjustments (e.g., taxes, fees, discounts) applied to the gross amount.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or authority that approved the change order.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the change order received approval.']
  change_order_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the change order by the contract administration system.']
  change_order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the change order purpose, e.g., addition, omission, time extension, price adjustment, or scope change.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or remarks entered by users regarding the change order.']
  contract_change_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the change order.']
  cost_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary impact of the change order on the contract price (positive for additions, negative for omissions).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the change order record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for monetary values.']
  dlp_end_date date [note: 'Date when the Defects Liability Period expires for work covered by the change order.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the change order becomes contractually effective.']
  eot_claim_reference varchar [note: 'Link to any Extension of Time claim associated with the schedule impact.']
  executed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the change order was executed and became binding.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount before any adjustments, taxes, or discounts.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the change order is considered critical to project delivery.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the change order record.']
  ld_provision_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount stipulated for liquidated damages if the change order affects performance penalties.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final payable amount after adjustments.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why the change order was initiated.']
  revised_completion_date date [note: 'New contract completion date after applying schedule impact.']
  schedule_impact_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the contract schedule is extended or reduced due to the change order.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the work scope affected by the change order.']
  submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the change order was formally submitted for approval.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the change order record.']
  variation_instruction_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the original variation instruction that triggered this change order.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the change order record.']

  Note: 'Authoritative log of all CO (Change Orders) and contract variations issued against a contract. Records variation instruction reference, scope description, reason code (employer-directed, unforeseen conditions, design change), cost impact (addition or omission), schedule impact in days, approval status, and executed value. Tracks the full variation lifecycle from instruction through valuation, negotiation, and formal execution. Links to EOT claims where schedule impact is asserted.'
}

Table contract.eot_claim {
  eot_claim_id varchar [pk, note: 'System generated unique identifier for the EOT claim record.']
  affected_contract_milestone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_milestone. Business justification: An EOT (Extension of Time) claim is submitted to extend the date of a specific contractual milestone — typically the completion milestone. contract_eot_claim has claim_original_schedule_date and claim']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the underlying contract to which this EOT claim relates.']
  authority_notice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.authority_notice. Business justification: Authority notices (stop-work orders, permit suspensions, enforcement notices) are primary delay events substantiating EOT claims. Construction contracts explicitly recognize regulatory authority actio']
  claim_related_ncr_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of a Non‑Conformance Report linked to the claim.']
  claim_related_rfi_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of a Request for Information that is associated with the claim.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the construction project associated with the claim.']
  contract_change_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of a change order that may be linked to the time extension request.']
  delay_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to schedule.delay_event. Business justification: EOT claims are substantiated by specific delay events recorded in the schedule. In construction contract administration, every EOT claim must reference the causative delay event(s) for entitlement ass']
  fleet_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.fleet_assignment. Business justification: EOT claims are frequently substantiated by evidence of equipment non-availability or delayed mobilization. Linking an EOT claim to the specific fleet assignment that caused or contributed to the delay']
  incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.incident. Business justification: EOT claims in construction are frequently caused by safety incidents triggering stop-work orders or work suspension. Construction claims managers must reference the causative incident record to substa']
  maintenance_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.maintenance_order. Business justification: Equipment breakdown requiring emergency maintenance is a recognized EOT entitlement event. Linking an EOT claim to the causative maintenance order provides documentary evidence for the claim, supports']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the contractor submitting the EOT claim.']
  project_milestone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_milestone. Business justification: EOT claims reference specific project schedule milestones that were delayed. claim_impact_on_milestones is currently a text field. Direct FK to project_milestone enables quantitative delay analysis, s']
  work_front_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.work_front. Business justification: EOT claims arise from delay events at specific work fronts. Linking enables delay analysis by work front, supports EOT substantiation reports, and allows site managers to identify which work fronts ar']
  claim_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Financial value of the claim based on days claimed and daily rate.']
  claim_approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who approved or rejected the claim.']
  claim_assessor_comments varchar [note: 'Free‑text comments entered by the engineer or assessor during evaluation.']
  claim_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the claim record was first created in the system.']
  claim_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the claim amount.']
  claim_decision_date date [note: 'Date when the final decision on the claim was recorded.']
  claim_external_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number used by external parties (e.g., client or auditor) to identify the claim.']
  claim_final_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final monetary amount after any adjustments or partial approvals.']
  claim_final_days varchar [note: 'Total number of days finally approved for the extension.']
  claim_impact_on_milestones varchar [note: 'Explanation of how the extension affects key project milestones.']
  claim_is_critical boolean [note: 'True if the claim is deemed critical to project delivery or financial exposure.']
  claim_new_schedule_date date [note: 'Projected completion date after applying the approved extension.']
  claim_notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes captured by any stakeholder.']
  claim_number varchar [note: 'Unique claim number assigned by the contractor or system for tracking.']
  claim_original_schedule_date date [note: 'Baseline project completion date before any extensions.']
  claim_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the claim for processing urgency.']
  claim_resolution_date date [note: 'Date when the claim was fully closed and any adjustments were posted.']
  claim_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the claim was formally reviewed by the project engineer.']
  claim_source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system where the claim was first recorded.']
  claim_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the claim within the claim lifecycle.']
  claim_status_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for the current status, such as reason for rejection or hold.']
  claim_submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the contractor formally submitted the EOT claim.']
  claim_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the contractual basis for the time extension request.']
  claim_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the claim record.']
  days_assessed varchar [note: 'Number of days approved by the engineer after assessment.']
  days_claimed varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the contractor claims as an extension.']
  delay_event_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the event that caused the schedule delay.']
  determination_outcome varchar [note: 'Final decision result after engineering review.']
  entitlement_basis varchar [note: 'FIDIC sub‑clause or contractual provision that justifies the claim.']
  liquidated_damages_impact decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated reduction in liquidated damages exposure due to the approved extension.']
  revised_completion_date date [note: 'New projected project completion date after the approved extension.']
  supporting_document_refs varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of document IDs (e.g., drawings, logs, photos) that substantiate the claim.']

  Note: 'Records EOT (Extension of Time) claims submitted by the contractor asserting entitlement to additional time due to employer risk events, force majeure, or other contractual grounds. Captures claim submission date, delay event description, days claimed, days assessed by engineer, grounds of entitlement (FIDIC Sub-Clause reference), supporting documentation references, and final determination outcome. Critical for managing LD (Liquidated Damages) exposure and revised completion date.'
}

Table contract.ld_assessment {
  ld_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each liquidated damages assessment record.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the contract to which this LD assessment applies.']
  payment_certificate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payment certificate from which the LD amount will be deducted.']
  project_milestone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_milestone. Business justification: LD assessments are triggered by delays to specific project schedule milestones (is_ld_trigger flag on project_milestone). Direct FK enables LD calculation based on actual vs planned milestone dates fr']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the milestone was actually completed, as recorded on site.']
  assessed_by varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or team that performed the LD assessment.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Date on which the LD assessment was formally recorded.']
  assessment_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the LD assessment, typically following the contract\'s numbering scheme.']
  assessment_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation for why the LD assessment was generated (e.g., missed milestone, force majeure).']
  assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the LD assessment.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the LD amounts (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  delay_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the milestone was delayed beyond the due date.']
  ld_rate_per_day decimal(12,2) [note: 'Contractual LD rate applied for each day of delay, expressed in the contract currency.']
  ld_waiver_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a waiver has been granted for this assessment (true = waiver granted).']
  ld_waiver_justification varchar [note: 'Explanation or contractual clause supporting the granted waiver.']
  milestone_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date by which the milestone was contractually required to be achieved.']
  milestone_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the contractual milestone that was missed (e.g., "Substantial Completion").']
  net_ld_deducted decimal(15,2) [note: 'Final liquidated damages amount deducted from the payment certificate after waivers.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or observations related to the assessment.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the LD assessment record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the LD assessment record.']
  total_ld_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Gross liquidated damages amount before any waivers or reductions.']
  waiver_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount of LD that has been waived or reduced per contractual negotiation.']

  Note: 'Tracks LD (Liquidated Damages) assessments applied or withheld against a contractor for failure to achieve contractual completion milestones. Records the milestone missed, LD rate per day (as defined in the contract appendix), number of days in delay, total LD amount assessed, any LD waiver or reduction granted, and the net LD deducted from payment certificates. Provides the authoritative LD register for financial reporting and dispute management.'
}

Table contract.retention_ledger {

  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the contract to which this retention record belongs.']
  contract_milestone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_milestone. Business justification: contract_retention_ledger currently stores contract_milestone_code as a denormalized STRING. Retention release events are directly tied to milestone achievement (e.g., 50% retention released at practi']
  payment_certificate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment certificate that triggered the retention calculation.']
  contract_retention_ledger_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the retention ledger entry.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the ledger record was first inserted.']
  cumulative_retention_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Running total of retention still held after this release.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for all monetary values in this record.']
  effective_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact point in time when the retention entry became effective.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or observations related to the entry.']
  release_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of retention released in this transaction (partial or full).']
  release_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the retention amount was released.']
  release_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for why the retention was released (e.g., milestone achieved, DLP expiry).']
  retention_adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary adjustment to the retention (positive or negative) applied after initial calculation.']
  retention_adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why a retention adjustment was made.']
  retention_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount retained for this entry before any releases.']
  retention_bond_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the retention bond when substitution occurs.']
  retention_bond_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the retention bond amount.']
  retention_bond_substituted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a retention bond was used in place of cash retention.']
  retention_entry_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable sequential identifier for the retention entry, often used in reports and certificates.']
  retention_hold_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when a retention hold is scheduled to be lifted if unresolved.']
  retention_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the retention amount is currently on hold (e.g., due to dispute).']
  retention_hold_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the retention is being held.']
  retention_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the payment amount retained as per contract terms (e.g., 5.00%).']
  retention_release_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the total retention released in this transaction.']
  retention_release_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal number indicating the order of releases for a given contract.']
  retention_release_type varchar [note: 'Reason category for the retention release (e.g., practical completion, DLP expiry).']
  retention_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the retention amount (e.g., regular payment certificate, change order, or manual adjustment).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the ledger record.']

  Note: 'Tracks the accumulation and release of retention monies held under a contract. Records retention percentage applied per payment certificate, cumulative retention balance, practical completion release amount (typically 50% of retention), DLP (Defects Liability Period) expiry release amount, retention bond substitution details, and any partial releases approved. Provides the authoritative retention balance for financial reconciliation.'
}

Formal contract amendments frequently modify specific milestone dates (e.g., revised completion date, DLP end date). Addin']
  schedule_baseline_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to schedule.schedule_baseline. Business justification: Contract amendments that extend time or revise scope require a revised schedule baseline. Linking amendment to the resulting schedule_baseline enables contract administrators to track which programme ']
  amendment_category varchar [note: 'High-level category of impact caused by the amendment.']

  amendment_type varchar [note: 'Category of amendment indicating its nature.']

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the amendment record was created in the system.']

  signed_date date [note: 'Date when the amendment was signed by the parties.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Source system where the amendment originated.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the amendment record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the amendment record for change tracking.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the amendment record.']

  Note: 'Records formal amendments to the original contract agreement, including supplemental agreements, deed of variations, and novation agreements. Captures amendment number, amendment type, effective date, description of changes to original terms, revised contract value, revised completion date, and execution status. Maintains a complete amendment history for each contract to support audit and dispute resolution.'
}

Linking subcontract to contract_scope establishes the fo']

  technical_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.technical_specification.   vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Links subcontract vendor to master vendor for compliance, insurance, and bonding checks.']

  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the subcontract ends or expires; null for open‑ended agreements.']

  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms agreed for the subcontract.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative risk rating assigned to the subcontract.']
  scope_summary varchar [note: 'Brief textual description of the work scope covered by the subcontract.']
  subcontract_number varchar [note: 'External contract number or code assigned to the subcontract as defined in the main contract documents.']
  subcontract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the subcontract.']
  subcontract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the subcontract based on procurement approach (e.g., domestic, nominated, specialist, trade).']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the subcontract record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the subcontract record.']
  value_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the subcontract as agreed in the contract.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the subcontract record.']

  Note: 'Master record for subcontracts let by the main contractor to specialist trade and MEP (Mechanical Electrical Plumbing) subcontractors under the main contract. Captures subcontract type (domestic, nominated, specialist), subcontract value, scope summary, back-to-back terms with main contract, NTP date, completion date, and subcontract status. Distinct from the subcontractor domain master — this entity owns the contractual instrument, not the subcontractor party profile.'
}

Table contract.subcontract_payment {
  subcontract_payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the subcontract payment record.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the master contract under which the subcontract work is performed.']
  subcontract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.subcontract. Business justification: subcontract_payment is the transactional payment record issued under a specific subcontract. Every payment application and certificate belongs to exactly one subcontract. Currently subcontract_payment']
  vendor_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_invoice. Business justification: Subcontract payments are processed against vendor invoices. Linking subcontract_payment to vendor_invoice enables payment reconciliation, audit trail, and accounts payable matching — essential for con']
  actual_payment_date date [note: 'Date the payment was actually received by the subcontractor.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of taxes, fees, or other adjustments applied to the gross amount.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the payment amounts.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which the payment is contractually due.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount claimed before any deductions.']
  is_late_payment boolean [note: 'True if the actual payment date is later than the due date.']
  is_ld_deduction_applied boolean [note: 'True when liquidated damages have been deducted from the payment.']
  is_retention_applied boolean [note: 'True when a retention amount is held back from the payment.']
  ld_deduction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount deducted for liquidated damages, if applicable.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount payable after all adjustments and deductions.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for any additional comments or explanations.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'Interface through which the payment was processed.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Date the payment was issued to the subcontractor.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument used to make the payment.']
  payment_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible identifier assigned to the payment application (e.g., PAY-2023-00123).']
  payment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payment (e.g., progress payment, final payment).']
  retention_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the payment retained per contract terms.']
  retention_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Retention expressed as a percentage of the gross amount.']
  subcontract_payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the payment record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the payment record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of payment applications and payment certificates issued under each subcontract. Captures application amount, certified amount, retention deducted, LD deductions, net payment due, payment due date, and actual payment date. Enables back-to-back payment flow management between main contract receipts and subcontractor disbursements, supporting cash flow forecasting and pay-when-paid clause administration.'
}

Table contract.closeout {
  closeout_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each contract closeout record.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original contract that is being closed out.']
  compliance_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.assessment. Business justification: Contract closeout checklists in construction require a compliance assessment sign-off (environmental audit, regulatory clearance) before the final certificate is issued. Role-prefixed \'compliance_asse']
  contract_milestone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_milestone. Business justification: In construction contracts, practical completion is a formal contractual milestone that triggers DLP commencement, retention release, and the closeout process. closeout has practical_completion_date bu']
  document_register_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary document package associated with the closeout.']
  final_payment_certificate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.payment_certificate. Business justification: closeout currently stores final_payment_certificate_number as a denormalized STRING. The contract closeout process is formally concluded with the issuance of a Final Payment Certificate (FPC). Adding ']
  final_vendor_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_invoice.
