Linking correspondence to reg']
  rfi_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.rfi. Business justification: Formal project correspondence is frequently raised in response to or in connection with an RFI. Adding rfi_id FK to correspondence enables traceability between correspondence items and the RFIs they r']
  sender_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.account. Business justification: Required for client communication log linking each sent correspondence to the originating client account.']
  transmittal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.transmittal. Business justification: Formal project correspondence is dispatched via transmittals in construction document management systems (e.g., Aconex). The correspondence table carries transmittal_number as a STRING field. Adding t']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.wbs_element. Business justification: Construction correspondence (formal letters, notices) is tagged to WBS elements for cost and claim management. A contract administrator tracks correspondence by WBS scope for variation and dispute res']
  attachment_count varchar [note: 'Number of document attachments associated with this correspondence item (drawings, specifications, photos, reports).']
  body_text varchar [note: 'Full text content of the correspondence message or letter body.']
  closure_date date [note: 'The date the correspondence thread was formally closed. Nullable if still open.']
  closure_status varchar [note: 'Final closure status indicating whether the correspondence thread was accepted, rejected, or superseded by subsequent correspondence.']
  confidential_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this correspondence is marked as confidential and subject to restricted access controls.']
  correspondence_date date [note: 'The date the correspondence was officially issued or sent. This is the principal business event timestamp for the correspondence lifecycle.']
  correspondence_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the correspondence item as registered in the Aconex mail register. This is the official reference number used in contractual communications.']
  correspondence_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the correspondence item tracking its workflow state from draft through closure.']
  correspondence_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the correspondence item. RFI (Request for Information), instruction, notice, claim, letter, or memo.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this correspondence record was first created in the document management system.']
  discipline varchar [note: 'Engineering or construction discipline to which this correspondence relates (civil, structural, MEP, architectural, etc.). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: civil|structural|mechanical|electrical|architectural|plumbing|general — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  distribution_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of additional parties or roles who were copied on this correspondence for information or action.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who last modified this correspondence record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this correspondence record was last modified or updated.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the correspondence indicating urgency of response or action required.']
  received_date date [note: 'The date the correspondence was received and registered in the document management system.']
  recipient_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the organization receiving the correspondence (contractor, client, consultant, subcontractor, or authority).']
  response_date date [note: 'The date a formal response was issued for this correspondence. Nullable if no response has been provided.']
  response_due_date date [note: 'The contractual or agreed date by which a response to this correspondence must be provided. Nullable if no response is required.']
  response_method varchar [note: 'The method or channel through which the response was delivered (email, formal letter, transmittal, meeting minutes, phone call).']
  response_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether a formal response is required for this correspondence.']
  response_text varchar [note: 'Full text content of the response provided to this correspondence. Nullable if no response has been issued.']
  sender_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the sender for correspondence tracking and response routing.']
  sender_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who authored or sent the correspondence.']
  subject varchar [note: 'Subject line or title of the correspondence describing the topic or issue being communicated.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who created this correspondence record.']

  Note: 'Master register of all formal project correspondence including letters, emails, notices, and official communications managed through Aconex mail register. Tracks sender, recipient, subject, correspondence type (RFI, instruction, notice, claim), priority, response required flag, response due date, current status, response thread history with full response chain (response date, content, method, closure status), and parent-child threading. Serves as the authoritative SSOT for all project correspondence and response chains across contractors, clients, consultants, and authorities, providing the contractual audit trail required under FIDIC and NEC contract forms.'
}

Table design.transmittal {
  transmittal_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the transmittal record. Primary key for the transmittal entity.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the contract or agreement under which this transmittal is issued. Links document distribution to contractual obligations and scope.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project for which this transmittal is issued. Links transmittal to the parent project context.']
  project_milestone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_milestone. Business justification: Transmittals are issued to satisfy contractual milestone document submission requirements (e.g., transmittal of IFC drawings triggers a milestone). A construction PM tracks transmittal issuance agains']
  recipient_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.contact. Business justification: Transmittals in construction document control are formally addressed to a named client contact. recipient_contact_name and recipient_email are plain-text denormalizations of client.contact. A FK enabl']
  regulatory_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_submission. Business justification: Transmittals are the formal mechanism for submitting design documents to regulatory authorities as part of regulatory submissions. Linking transmittal to regulatory_submission enables tracking of whic']
  sender_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.account. Business justification: Transmittal origin must be linked to the client account for contract document tracking and regulatory filing.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.wbs_element. Business justification: Transmittals are issued against specific WBS elements in construction document management for scope traceability. A document controller tracks which WBS scope each transmittal covers for contract comp']
  acknowledgement_by varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who provided formal acknowledgement on behalf of the recipient organization. Establishes personal accountability for receipt confirmation.']
  acknowledgement_date date [note: 'The date when the recipient formally acknowledged receipt of the transmittal. Null if not yet acknowledged. Provides legally defensible proof of document delivery.']
  acknowledgement_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator specifying whether the recipient is required to formally acknowledge receipt of the transmittal. True indicates acknowledgement is mandatory.']
  acknowledgement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of recipient acknowledgement tracking. Indicates whether the transmittal has been formally acknowledged by the recipient organization.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level indicating the sensitivity and access restrictions applicable to the transmittal and its enclosed documents.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the transmittal record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for record origination.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'The method or channel used to physically or electronically deliver the transmittal to the recipient. Tracks distribution logistics.']
  discipline varchar [note: 'The primary engineering or design discipline to which the transmittal documents belong. Categorizes transmittals by technical specialization for routing and review. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: architectural|structural|mechanical|electrical|plumbing|civil|geotechnical|environmental — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  document_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual documents or drawings included in this transmittal package. Used for completeness verification and audit trail.']
  due_date date [note: 'The date by which the recipient is expected to respond or take action on the transmittal, particularly relevant for approval or review purposes. Null if no response deadline applies.']
  issue_date date [note: 'The date when the transmittal was formally issued and dispatched to the recipient organization. Represents the principal business event timestamp for document distribution.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the transmittal record was last modified or updated. Tracks the most recent change to the record for audit and version control purposes.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the transmittal indicating the urgency of review or response required from the recipient. Guides workflow prioritization.']
  purpose_of_issue varchar [note: 'The intended purpose or action required from the recipient upon receiving the transmittal. Defines the business intent behind the document distribution and guides recipient response obligations.']
  recipient_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or company receiving the transmittal. Identifies the party to whom the documents are being dispatched.']
  reference_transmittal_number varchar [note: 'Transmittal number of a previous or related transmittal that this transmittal references, supersedes, or responds to. Establishes document lineage and traceability.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the transmittal that do not fit in other structured fields. Provides supplementary context.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Revision identifier for the transmittal itself if it has been reissued or updated. Tracks version history of the transmittal record.']
  sender_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual within the sender organization who prepared and issued the transmittal. Provides personal accountability for document distribution.']
  sender_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the sender contact for correspondence and queries related to the transmittal.']
  subject varchar [note: 'Brief descriptive title or subject line summarizing the content and purpose of the transmittal. Provides quick context for document package identification.']
  transmittal_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the transmittal contents, context, and any special instructions or notes for the recipient. Provides comprehensive background information.']
  transmittal_number varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the transmittal as recognized by all project parties. Typically follows project-specific numbering conventions and serves as the externally-known reference for document distribution tracking.']
  transmittal_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the transmittal indicating its position in the document distribution workflow. Tracks progression from draft through issuance to acknowledgement or closure.']

  Note: 'Records the formal dispatch of documents, drawings, and submittals between project parties via Aconex transmittals. Captures transmittal number, issue date, sender organization, recipient organization, purpose of issue (for approval, for information, for construction, for record), document list, and acknowledgement status. Provides a legally defensible audit trail of document distribution throughout the project lifecycle.'
}

Table design.rfi {
  rfi_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for rfi']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Supports RFI‑to‑Agreement traceability required in the RFI Management Process and impact assessment on contract scope.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.asset. Business justification: Equipment-related RFIs are a named construction process — queries about a specific asset\'s load capacity, installation specs, or operational parameters. A construction engineer expects rfi to referenc']
  client_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.contact. Business justification: RFIs in construction are formally directed to a specific client contact (e.g., client\'s design manager or engineer) who is contractually responsible for responding. Tracking the client contact on an R']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project to which this RFI belongs.']
  craft_worker_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.craft_worker. Business justification: RFI is raised by a craft worker on site to request clarification; linking captures who raised it (RFI Management Process).']
  drawing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.drawing. Business justification: RFIs in construction are most commonly raised about specific drawings where clarification is needed on design intent. Adding drawing_id FK to rfi enables direct linkage between an RFI and the drawing ']
  finance_cost_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_code. Business justification: RFIs may result in cost changes; linking each RFI to the relevant cost code enables cost impact analysis.']
  package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.package. Business justification: RFIs are raised against specific design packages, seeking clarification on the content of a package issued at a project milestone. Adding package_id FK to rfi establishes this relationship, enabling a']
  permit_condition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit_condition. Business justification: RFIs often seek clarification of specific permit conditions; linking RFI to the condition it addresses enables compliance tracking.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: RFIs are raised to clarify regulatory obligations affecting design (which standard applies, what evidence is required, how an obligation is interpreted). Linking RFI to regulatory_obligation enables c']
  specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to material.specification. Business justification: RFIs in construction are frequently raised to clarify or challenge material specifications (substitution requests, grade clarifications). Linking an RFI to the specific material specification being qu']
  technical_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.technical_specification. Business justification: RFIs are frequently raised to seek clarification on technical specifications — material requirements, workmanship standards, testing requirements. Adding technical_specification_id FK to rfi enables d']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the WBS element or work package to which this RFI is associated for cost and schedule tracking.']
  work_front_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.work_front. Business justification: RFIs are raised from specific work fronts where a design clarification is needed to proceed. RFI management reports and site instruction workflows require knowing which work front generated the RFI to']
  actual_response_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the RFI response was provided and recorded in the system.']
  closure_date date [note: 'The date on which the RFI was formally closed after response acceptance and any required follow-up actions were completed.']
  closure_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments recorded at the time of RFI closure, documenting final resolution or outstanding items.']
  cost_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated financial impact (in project currency) resulting from the RFI clarification, if applicable.']
  cost_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the RFI response has identified a potential cost impact to the project, triggering change order evaluation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this RFI record was first created in the data platform.']
  date_raised date [note: 'The date on which the RFI was formally submitted or raised in the project management system.']
  discipline varchar [note: 'The engineering or design discipline to which this RFI pertains (e.g., architectural, structural, MEP). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: architectural|structural|mechanical|electrical|plumbing|civil|geotechnical — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this RFI in the source system, used for traceability and reconciliation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this RFI record was last updated in the data platform, supporting audit trail and change tracking.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the RFI indicating urgency and impact on project schedule or critical path.']
  response_content varchar [note: 'Detailed text of the response provided by the addressee, including clarifications, instructions, or references to supporting documentation.']
  response_due_date date [note: 'The contractual or agreed-upon date by which a response to the RFI is required, often tied to project schedule milestones.']
  response_time_days varchar [note: 'Calculated number of calendar days between date raised and actual response date, used for performance tracking and SLA compliance.']
  rfi_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the question, issue, or clarification being requested, including context and specific areas of concern.']
  rfi_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the RFI, typically a sequential or structured code used for tracking and reference in project documentation and correspondence.']
  rfi_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the RFI indicating its position in the review and response workflow.']
  schedule_impact_days varchar [note: 'Estimated number of calendar days of schedule delay resulting from the RFI clarification, if applicable.']
  schedule_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the RFI response has identified a potential schedule impact, potentially triggering an Extension of Time (EOT) request.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system from which this RFI record was sourced (e.g., Procore RFI module, Aconex correspondence).']
  subject varchar [note: 'Brief title or subject line summarizing the nature of the clarification request.']

  Note: 'Request for Information records capturing formal queries raised by contractors seeking clarification on design intent, specifications, or contract documents. Tracks RFI number, subject, discipline, originator, addressee, date raised, response due date, actual response date, response content, cost/schedule impact assessment, linked drawing or specification reference, and closure status. High-volume transactional entity critical for design coordination and contractual record-keeping. Sourced from Procore RFI module and Aconex.'
}

Table design.document_register {
  document_register_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the document register entry. Primary key for the document register product.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the contract under which this document was produced or to which it is a deliverable.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project this document belongs to.']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Permit Application requires linking each design document to the specific permit it satisfies for regulatory approval.']
  rfi_document_rfi_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.rfi. Business justification: RFI is currently siloed; adding a foreign key from document_register to rfi creates an inbound link and removes the redundant string reference column.']
  transmittal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.transmittal. Business justification: Documents in the document register are issued and distributed via transmittals. The document_register table carries transmittal_number as a STRING field. Adding transmittal_id FK normalizes this to a ']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the WBS element or work package this document supports or relates to.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the document received formal approval from the designated approver.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who provided final approval for the document to be issued.']
  confidentiality_classification varchar [note: 'Security classification level of the document content determining access rights and distribution restrictions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this document register record was first created in the system.']
  discipline varchar [note: 'Engineering or construction discipline responsible for the document. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: civil|structural|architectural|mechanical|electrical|plumbing|instrumentation|controls|process|geotechnical|environmental|HSE|quality|project_management — promote to reference product]']
  distribution_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of parties, roles, or organizations to whom this document should be distributed.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the document following project numbering convention. This is the externally-known document reference used across all project communications and systems.']
  document_purpose varchar [note: 'Brief statement describing the intended use and purpose of the document within the project context.']
  document_register_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the document in the approval and distribution workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|in_review|approved|issued_for_construction|issued_for_information|issued_for_tender|superseded|archived|void|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  document_title varchar [note: 'Full descriptive title of the document as it appears on the document itself.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the document by its primary purpose and content type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: specification|report|procedure|method_statement|manual|certificate|correspondence|transmittal|inspection_plan|quality_plan|test_report|safety_plan|environmental_plan|commissioning_plan|handover_document|as_built_record|warranty|permit — promote to reference product]']
  file_format varchar [note: 'Digital file format of the document (e.g., PDF for specifications, DWG for CAD drawings, RVT for Revit models). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PDF|DOCX|XLSX|DWG|RVT|IFC|XML — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  file_size_mb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Size of the document file in megabytes for storage and transmission planning.']
  is_client_deliverable boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this document is a contractual deliverable to the client requiring formal acceptance.']
  is_controlled_document boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this document is subject to formal change control procedures and requires approval for any modifications.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date when the current revision of the document was formally issued or published.']
  keywords varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of keywords or tags for document search and categorization purposes.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 639-2 code representing the primary language of the document content. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ENG|SPA|FRA|DEU|ARA|CHI|POR — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this document register record was last updated or modified.']
  originator varchar [note: 'Organization or party responsible for creating and issuing the document (e.g., design consultant, contractor, subcontractor, client).']
  page_count varchar [note: 'Total number of pages in the document for printing and review planning purposes.']
  retention_period_years varchar [note: 'Number of years the document must be retained per regulatory, contractual, or organizational requirements before eligible for disposal.']
  review_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the document review must be completed to maintain project schedule.']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who reviewed the document for technical accuracy and completeness.']
  revision_description varchar [note: 'Brief description of the changes made in the current revision compared to the previous version.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Current revision identifier of the document following project revision convention (e.g., A, B, C or 01, 02, 03 or R0, R1, R2).']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Physical or digital location where the document is stored (e.g., Aconex folder path, SharePoint site, physical archive location).']
  superseded_by_document_number varchar [note: 'Document number of the newer document that supersedes this document, if applicable. Used to maintain document lineage and version control.']
  supersedes_document_number varchar [note: 'Document number of the older document that this document supersedes or replaces.']

  Note: 'Central master register of all project documents beyond drawings — including specifications, reports, procedures, method statements, O&M manuals, certificates, and correspondence — managed in Aconex CDE (Common Data Environment). Captures document number, title, document type, discipline, revision, status, originator, issue date, confidentiality classification, retention period, and numbering convention. Provides the authoritative catalog of all project documentation per ISO 19650 information container requirements.'
}

Table design.revision {
  revision_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for revision']
  document_register_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent document in the document register. Links this revision to its master document record.']
  ncr_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.ncr. Business justification: Drawing and document revisions are frequently issued specifically to address NCRs requiring design correction. Linking revision to the triggering NCR enables NCR closure tracking and demonstrates desi']
  superseded_revision_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the previous revision that this version replaces. Maintains the revision chain and version history.']
  transmittal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.transmittal. Business justification: Each document revision is distributed to project parties via a formal transmittal. The revision table carries transmittal_number as a STRING field. Adding transmittal_id FK normalizes this to a proper']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when this revision was officially approved for issuance. Represents a distinct lifecycle event in the approval workflow.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who approved this revision for release. Authorized signatory for document issuance.']
  author_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who authored or prepared this revision. Responsible party for the content creation.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Primary reason or trigger for this revision. Categorizes the business driver behind the document update.']
  change_summary varchar [note: 'Concise summary of the specific changes made in this revision compared to the previous version. Highlights key modifications, additions, or deletions.']
  checksum_hash varchar [note: 'Cryptographic hash (e.g., SHA-256) of the file content. Ensures file integrity and detects unauthorized modifications.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional notes, remarks, or instructions related to this revision. Captures supplementary information not covered by other fields.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this revision record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  distribution_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of recipients or distribution groups who should receive this revision. Tracks who needs to be notified of the update.']
  file_format varchar [note: 'File type or format of the document (e.g., PDF, DWG for CAD, RVT for BIM). Indicates the software required to open the file. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pdf|dwg|dxf|rvt|ifc|docx|xlsx|pptx — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  file_name varchar [note: 'Name of the physical file associated with this revision, including extension (e.g., Drawing_A101_RevB.pdf).']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the document file in bytes. Used for storage management and download estimation.']
  file_storage_path varchar [note: 'Full path or URI to the physical file location in the document management system or cloud storage. Enables retrieval of the actual document file.']
  is_controlled_copy boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this revision is a controlled copy subject to formal change management. True for official project copies, false for reference or uncontrolled copies.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this revision record was last updated in the system. Audit trail for record modifications.']
  page_count varchar [note: 'Total number of pages in this revision. Used for printing, archiving, and completeness verification.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date when the review of this revision was completed. Represents a distinct lifecycle event in the approval workflow.']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who reviewed this revision for technical accuracy and completeness.']
  revision_date date [note: 'Date when this revision was officially issued or published. Represents the business event timestamp for the revision release.']
  revision_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the purpose and scope of this revision. Explains what changed and why.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential or alphanumeric identifier for this revision (e.g., Rev A, Rev 01, R1.2). Represents the version sequence within the document lifecycle.']
  revision_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this revision. Tracks the approval and publication workflow state.']
  revision_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the revision based on the nature and impact of changes. Distinguishes between routine updates and significant modifications.']
  sheet_count varchar [note: 'Total number of sheets or drawings in this revision (applicable for CAD and BIM documents). Used for drawing set completeness checks.']

  Note: 'Version control record for each document in the document register, capturing revision identifier, revision date, revision description, author, reviewer, approver, change summary, superseded revision reference, and file storage path. Maintains a complete and auditable version history for all project documents to support regulatory compliance, handover, and DLP (Defects Liability Period) obligations.'
}

Table design.workflow_approval {
  workflow_approval_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the workflow approval instance. Primary key for the workflow approval entity.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Enables linking workflow approvals to the specific contract for regulatory compliance and audit trails.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project to which this workflow approval belongs. Enables project-level reporting and audit trails.']
  document_register_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the document or deliverable under review in this workflow. Links to the document management system record.']
  phase_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.phase. Business justification: Approval workflows in construction are phase-specific (FEED approval workflows vs. IFC approval workflows). A project manager reports approval SLA compliance and bottlenecks by phase for gate review d']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Workflow approvals are frequently triggered by regulatory obligations requiring mandatory third-party or authority review of design documents. Compliance teams must verify all obligatory approval work']
  revision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.revision. Business justification: A workflow approval instance is initiated for a specific document revision in the document control system. The workflow_approval table carries revision_number as a STRING field. Adding revision_id FK ']
  action_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the reviewer recorded their approval decision or action. Used for SLA compliance tracking and audit trail.']
  action_taken varchar [note: 'The decision or action recorded by the reviewer at the current workflow step. Determines routing to next step or workflow termination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: approved|approved_with_comments|rejected|no_objection|commented|delegated|returned_for_revision|acknowledged|pending — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  approval_authority_level varchar [note: 'The organizational or contractual authority level required to approve this workflow. Determines who has final sign-off rights per delegation of authority matrix. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: project_team|discipline_lead|project_manager|design_manager|technical_director|client_representative|regulatory_authority — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  assigned_reviewer_role varchar [note: 'The functional role or position assigned to review the current step (e.g., Lead Engineer, Design Manager, QA/QC Manager, Project Director). Defines approval authority.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this workflow approval to the complete audit trail log in the document management system. Supports ISO 9001 and ISO 19650 compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this workflow approval record was first created in the data platform. Audit field for data lineage and record lifecycle tracking.']
  current_step_sequence varchar [note: 'The ordinal position of the current approval step in the workflow chain. Used to track progress and determine next routing action.']
  delegation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided by the original reviewer for delegating the approval to another individual (e.g., absence, conflict of interest, technical expertise).']
  due_date date [note: 'Target completion date for the entire workflow approval process. Derived from project schedule, contractual milestones, or regulatory deadlines.']
  escalation_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the workflow was escalated to higher approval authority or management attention due to delay or complexity.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether this workflow step was escalated to higher authority due to SLA breach, complexity, or policy requirements. True if escalated.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this workflow approval in the source system (Aconex workflow ID, BIM 360 review ID). Enables bi-directional traceability.']
  initiated_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the workflow approval was initiated and entered the approval chain. Marks the start of the approval lifecycle and SLA clock.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this workflow approval record was last updated in the data platform. Audit field for change tracking and data freshness monitoring.']
  last_reminder_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the most recent reminder notification was sent to the assigned reviewer. Used to schedule next reminder per escalation policy.']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether automated notification was sent to the assigned reviewer when the workflow step was routed to them. True if notification sent.']
  outcome_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the final workflow outcome was determined and the approval process was closed. Marks the end of the approval lifecycle.']
  overall_outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the complete workflow approval process after all steps are completed. Determines document status and next business actions.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the workflow approval. Influences SLA targets, routing urgency, and reviewer notification frequency.']
  regulatory_requirement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether this workflow approval is required to satisfy regulatory, statutory, or building code compliance obligations. True if regulatory-driven.']
  reminder_count varchar [note: 'Number of automated reminder notifications sent to the assigned reviewer for pending action. Used to track follow-up frequency and SLA risk.']
  reviewer_comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments, observations, or conditions recorded by the reviewer. Captures technical feedback, approval conditions, or reasons for rejection.']
  sla_actual_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The actual elapsed time in hours from workflow step assignment to action completion. Used to calculate SLA compliance and identify bottlenecks.']
  sla_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the workflow step was completed within the SLA target duration. True if compliant, False if breached.']
  sla_target_hours varchar [note: 'The contractual or policy-defined target duration in hours for completing this workflow step. Used to measure compliance with approval turnaround commitments.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this workflow approval instance originated (Aconex, BIM 360, Procore). Enables system-specific integration and audit.']
  total_steps varchar [note: 'Total number of approval steps defined in the workflow template. Used to calculate completion percentage and remaining steps.']
  workflow_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the workflow approval instance, typically derived from the document title or approval purpose.']
  workflow_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the workflow approval instance. Externally visible reference number used in correspondence and audit trails.']
  workflow_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the workflow approval. Tracks progression through the approval chain from initiation to final outcome. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: initiated|in_progress|pending_review|approved|rejected|on_hold|cancelled|completed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  workflow_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the workflow approval by business purpose. Determines routing rules, SLA targets, and approval authority requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: design_review|submittal_approval|rfi_response|change_order_approval|drawing_approval|specification_review|technical_query|document_transmittal|noncompliance_review|method_statement_approval — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Formal document review and approval workflow instances tracking multi-step approval chains in Aconex and BIM 360 CDE. Captures workflow template, document under review, step sequence, assigned reviewer (role and individual), action taken (approved, rejected, commented, delegated, no objection), action date, reviewer comments, SLA target vs actual duration, and overall workflow outcome. Provides the legally required audit trail of all document approval decisions for ISO 9001 quality management, ISO 19650 information management, and contractual compliance under FIDIC/NEC.'
}

  storage_location varchar [note: 'Physical or digital storage location where the handover package is archived (e.g., document management system path, archive facility).']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the handover package was submitted to the client or owner for review and acceptance.']

  Note: 'Master record for project handover and commissioning documentation packages compiled for client/owner delivery at practical completion per ISO 19650-3 operational phase requirements. Sourced from Aconex handover module.'
}

Table design.bim_model {
  bim_model_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the BIM model record. Primary key for the BIM model entity.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project this BIM model belongs to. Links the model to its parent project context.']
  env_impact_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.env_impact_assessment. Business justification: BIM models are scoped to EIA findings — design zones, lifecycle stages, and mitigation measures must align with EIA requirements. Compliance teams use BIM to verify EIA mitigation is reflected in desi']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_permit. Business justification: BIM models are produced under specific building or environmental permits. ISO 19650 compliance reporting requires linking BIM models to the permits governing the works. Permit scope verification uses ']
  superseded_by_model_bim_model_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer BIM model version that replaces this one. Null if this is the current version.']
  author_organization varchar [note: 'Organization or company responsible for authoring the BIM model (e.g., design consultant, contractor).']
  authoring_software varchar [note: 'Software application used to create and edit the BIM model (e.g., Autodesk Revit, Bentley MicroStation, ArchiCAD, Tekla Structures).']
  building_zone varchar [note: 'Specific building, zone, or facility area that this BIM model represents (e.g., Tower A, Basement Level 2, North Wing).']
  clash_count varchar [note: 'Number of unresolved clashes identified in the latest clash detection run. Zero indicates coordination clearance.']
  clash_detection_status varchar [note: 'Status of clash detection analysis for this model against other discipline models. Critical for coordination.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional notes, remarks, or special instructions related to the BIM model version.']
  confidentiality_classification varchar [note: 'Data classification level for the BIM model determining access controls and distribution restrictions.']
  coordinate_system varchar [note: 'Coordinate reference system used for model positioning (e.g., WGS84, NAD83, local grid). Ensures spatial alignment across disciplines.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this BIM model record was first created in the system.']
  discipline varchar [note: 'Engineering or design discipline that owns this BIM model. Determines the technical domain and responsible team. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: architectural|structural|mechanical|electrical|plumbing|civil|geotechnical|landscape|interior_design|fire_protection|telecommunications|environmental — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  element_count varchar [note: 'Total number of BIM elements (objects) contained in the model. Indicator of model complexity.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier from the source system (Autodesk BIM 360, Bentley ProjectWise) for integration and synchronization.']
  federation_role varchar [note: 'Role of this model in federated model assembly. Determines how it integrates with other discipline models.']
  file_format varchar [note: 'Native or exchange file format of the BIM model. Determines interoperability and viewing requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: RVT|NWD|NWC|IFC|DWG|DGN|SKP|RFA|DXF — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  file_size_mb decimal(12,2) [note: 'Size of the BIM model file in megabytes. Used for storage planning and performance optimization.']
  iso_19650_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the BIM model meets ISO 19650 information management requirements and standards.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date when this version of the BIM model was formally issued or published to the project team.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this BIM model record was last updated in the system.']
  lifecycle_stage varchar [note: 'Stage of the project lifecycle this model version represents. Aligns with ISO 19650 delivery phases. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: concept|design_development|construction_documentation|construction|commissioning|operations|decommissioning — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  lod_classification varchar [note: 'Level of Development per AIA E202 and BIMForum LOD Specification. Defines the degree of geometric detail and information richness in the model elements.']
  model_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the BIM model describing its content and scope (e.g., Terminal Building Level 1 Architecture, Bridge Deck Structural Model).']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the BIM model, typically following project naming conventions (e.g., ARC-L01-001, STR-F02-005). Used for external reference and communication.']
  model_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the BIM model. Controls access, usage, and workflow progression.']
  model_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the model purpose and usage context within the project lifecycle.']
  model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for this iteration of the BIM model. Tracks evolution and change history.']
  origin_elevation_m decimal(10,3) [note: 'Elevation of the model origin point in meters above sea level. Provides vertical datum reference.']
  origin_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Latitude coordinate of the model origin point in decimal degrees. Establishes geolocation reference.']
  origin_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Longitude coordinate of the model origin point in decimal degrees. Establishes geolocation reference.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date when the BIM model review was completed and approved for issue.']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who performed technical review of the BIM model before publication.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Formal revision number following project document control conventions (e.g., P01, C02, A03).']
  software_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the authoring software used to create the model. Critical for compatibility and file exchange.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'File path or cloud storage location where the BIM model file is stored (e.g., BIM 360 project folder, SharePoint path).']
  wbs_code varchar [note: 'Work Breakdown Structure code linking the BIM model to specific project scope and cost accounts.']

  Note: 'Master record for each Building Information Model (BIM) asset managed in Autodesk BIM 360, Bentley ProjectWise, or equivalent platform. Captures model identity, discipline (architectural, structural, MEP, civil), authoring software and version, file format (RVT, IFC, NWD), project coordinate system, geolocation reference point, LOD (Level of Development) classification per AIA E202/BIMForum LOD Specification, model status (WIP, shared, published, archived), lifecycle stage, federation role (host/linked), and file size. SSOT for all 3D model metadata across the project portfolio, enabling federated model assembly, clash detection workflows, 4D/5D BIM integration, and ISO 19650 information management compliance.'
}

  zone_location varchar [note: 'Physical zone, building, or location within the project site that the drawing depicts.']

  Note: 'Master record for each engineering and construction drawing (CAD/BIM-derived) managed across the project. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|in_review|approved|issued|superseded|void|archived — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  sheet_count varchar [note: 'Number of sheets or pages in this drawing revision. Relevant for multi-sheet drawings and printing logistics.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each revision event for a drawing. Enables full version-control audit trail for IFC/CAD drawing lifecycle.'
}

  specification_title varchar [note: 'Full descriptive title of the technical specification document, clearly identifying the scope of work, materials, or systems covered.']
  specification_type varchar [note: 'Classification of specification approach: performance-based (outcome-focused), prescriptive (method-focused), proprietary (brand-specific), reference (standard-based), or master (template).']

  superseded_date date [note: 'Date when this specification was replaced by a newer revision or cancelled, marking the end of its active lifecycle.']

  technical_specification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the technical specification document within the document control workflow.   testing_requirements varchar [note: 'Required tests, inspections, and acceptance criteria including FAT (Factory Acceptance Test), SAT (Site Acceptance Test), and ITP (Inspection and Test Plan) references.']
  warranty_period_months varchar [note: 'Duration in months for which materials, equipment, or workmanship covered by this specification must be warranted, typically aligned with DLP (Defects Liability Period).']
  workmanship_standards varchar [note: 'Standards and requirements for execution, installation, fabrication, and construction methods to ensure quality workmanship.']

  Note: 'Master record for each technical specification document governing materials, workmanship, and construction methods. Captures spec number, title, discipline, applicable standards (ACI, AISC, NFPA), revision status, approval state, and scope of work section reference. Linked to WBS elements and BOQ line items for design-build scope management.'
}

  phase_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.phase. Business justification: Design packages are organized by project phase (concept, FEED, detailed design, IFC). A design manager reports package completeness and submission status by phase for gate review decisions. No existin']

  planned_issue_date date [note: 'Originally scheduled date for package issuance as per project baseline schedule.']
  recipient_distribution_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of organizations or individuals to whom this package is distributed.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided by client or reviewer for package rejection or conditional acceptance.']
  responsible_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the organization (contractor, consultant, JV partner) responsible for preparing this package.']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or team who performed technical review of the package.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Revision identifier for this package version (e.g., Rev 0, Rev A, Rev 1).']
  specification_count varchar [note: 'Number of technical specifications included in this package.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'File system path, document management system location, or cloud storage URI where the package files are stored.']
  submission_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the package submission to client or reviewing authority.']
  supersedes_package_number varchar [note: 'Package number of the previous version that this package replaces or supersedes.']
  title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the design deliverable package indicating its scope and content.']
  transmittal_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the transmittal document used to formally issue this package.']

  Note: 'Master record representing a formal design deliverable package issued at a project milestone (30%, 60%, 90%, IFC, As-Built). Groups related drawings, specifications, calculations, and BIM models into a contractual issuance unit. Tracks package number, milestone stage, discipline, issue date, contractual due date, recipient distribution list, submission status, client acceptance status, and approval workflow state. Also serves as the contractual deliverable register (DDR), tracking each required deliverable item with its type (drawing, specification, report, model, calculation), responsible discipline, milestone linkage, planned vs. actual submission dates, and client acceptance status. SSOT for design deliverable scheduling, contractual compliance monitoring, and milestone gate readiness assessment.'
}

Adding technical_specificatio']

  actual_review_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the review was completed and the submittal disposition was communicated back to the submitting party.']
  actual_submission_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the submittal was formally submitted to the reviewing authority, used for schedule performance tracking and SLA compliance.']
  approval_authority_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the approval authority indicating the organizational level or role responsible for final disposition: contractor for internal review, design_consultant for technical design review, client for owner acceptance, regulatory_authority for statutory compliance, independent_verifier for third-party certification.']
  approval_disposition varchar [note: 'Final disposition code assigned by the reviewing authority indicating the outcome of the review: approved for full acceptance without changes, approved_as_noted for conditional acceptance with minor comments that do not require resubmission, revise_and_resubmit for rework and formal resubmission required, rejected for non-compliance requiring major revision, no_exception_taken for acknowledgment without formal approval, reviewed_for_information for informational submittals not requiring approval.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual with approval authority who formally authorized the submittal disposition, may be the same as reviewer or a senior authority depending on approval matrix.']
  attachment_count varchar [note: 'Number of supporting documents, drawings, data sheets, or files attached to this submittal, used for completeness verification and document control.']
  bim_model_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the BIM model element, object identifier, or model file that this submittal relates to, enabling integration between physical submittals and digital BIM coordination.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date on which the submittal was formally closed, indicating that all review cycles are complete, all comments are addressed, and the item is approved for construction or procurement.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level indicating the sensitivity and access restrictions for this submittal: public for unrestricted information, internal for company-internal use, confidential for business-sensitive information, restricted for highly sensitive or proprietary data.']
  cost_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this submittal has potential cost implications, such as value engineering proposals, material substitutions, or design changes affecting project budget.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when this submittal record was first created in the document management system, used for audit trail and record lifecycle tracking.']
  discipline varchar [note: 'Engineering or design discipline responsible for this submittal, used for routing and review assignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: architectural|structural|mechanical|electrical|plumbing|civil|geotechnical|environmental — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  estimated_cost_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated financial impact (positive or negative) associated with this submittal, expressed in the project base currency. Positive values indicate cost increases, negative values indicate savings.']
  file_format varchar [note: 'Primary file format of the submittal package, indicating the digital format used for submission (PDF for documents, DWG/DXF for CAD drawings, RVT for Revit models, IFC for BIM exchange, XLSX for spreadsheets, DOCX for text documents, ZIP for compressed packages). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PDF|DWG|DXF|RVT|IFC|XLSX|DOCX|ZIP — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording the most recent update to this submittal record, used for change tracking and audit trail purposes.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority classification indicating the urgency and schedule impact of this submittal: critical for items on the critical path requiring immediate review, high for near-term procurement or construction activities, medium for standard schedule items, low for long-lead or informational items.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory body or statutory authority whose approval or compliance verification is required for this submittal (e.g., local building department, fire marshal, environmental agency).']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this submittal requires verification of compliance with statutory regulations, building codes, or regulatory authority approvals (e.g., OSHA, EPA, local building department).']
  required_submission_date date [note: 'Contractually mandated or schedule-driven date by which the submittal must be submitted to the design team or client for review, typically derived from the project schedule and procurement lead times.']
  response_notes varchar [note: 'Response notes provided by the submitting party addressing reviewer comments, documenting how comments were incorporated or providing justification for alternative approaches.']
  review_comments varchar [note: 'Detailed technical comments, observations, and instructions provided by the reviewing authority during the review process, documenting required corrections, clarifications, or conditions of approval.']
  review_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the reviewing authority (design consultant, client representative) is expected to complete their review and return the submittal with disposition, typically governed by contract SLA terms.']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who performed the technical review and assigned the disposition for this submittal.']
  reviewing_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the organization responsible for reviewing and approving this submittal, typically the design consultant, architect, or client representative.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Revision identifier for this submittal, incremented with each resubmission following review comments or rejection, typically using alphanumeric convention (e.g., A, B, C or 01, 02, 03).']
  schedule_impact_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days by which the project schedule would be delayed if this submittal is not approved by the review due date, used for schedule risk analysis.']
  schedule_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether delays in reviewing or approving this submittal will impact the project critical path or key milestone dates.']
  submittal_number varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the submittal within the project, typically following a project-specific numbering convention (e.g., S-001, SUB-MEP-001).']
  submittal_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the submittal in the review and approval workflow. Draft indicates preparation phase, submitted indicates formal lodgment, under_review indicates active evaluation, approved indicates full acceptance, approved_as_noted indicates conditional acceptance with minor comments, revise_and_resubmit indicates rework required, rejected indicates non-compliance, withdrawn indicates contractor cancellation, superseded indicates replacement by newer revision. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|approved_as_noted|revise_and_resubmit|rejected|withdrawn|superseded — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  submittal_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the submittal item indicating the nature of the submission: shop drawings for fabrication details, product data sheets for material specifications, physical samples for approval, method statements for construction procedures, mix designs for concrete/asphalt, calculations for structural/MEP systems, or test reports for quality verification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: shop_drawing|product_data|sample|method_statement|mix_design|calculation|test_report — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  submitting_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the contractor, subcontractor, or supplier organization responsible for preparing and submitting this submittal.']
  supersedes_submittal_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the previous submittal number that this revision supersedes, establishing the revision chain and audit trail.']
  title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the submittal item identifying the material, product, or system being submitted for review.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for each design-phase submittal item tracking contractor-submitted shop drawings, material data sheets, product samples, and method statements through the review and approval lifecycle. Includes register-level metadata (specification section, required submission date, contractual obligation) and item-level tracking (submission date, review status, approval authority, disposition).'
}

  disposition varchar [note: 'Overall outcome or decision of the design review. Accepted means approved without changes; conditionally accepted requires minor revisions; rejected requires major rework; revise and resubmit requires resubmission after changes.']
  duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total duration of the design review meeting or event in hours.']

  minor_comments_count varchar [note: 'Number of minor comments raised that are recommended but not mandatory for approval.']
  minutes_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the formal meeting minutes or record document for this design review event.']
  next_review_scheduled_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next design review or follow-up review event.']
  recommendations varchar [note: 'Key recommendations or improvement suggestions arising from the design review.']
  review_date date [note: 'The date on which the formal design review event took place.']

  review_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the design review event.']
  review_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the design review event. Internal peer review is conducted within the design team; Interdisciplinary Check (IDC) validates coordination across disciplines; client milestone review is a formal client gate review; third-party review involves external consultants; authority review is conducted by regulatory bodies; constructability review assesses buildability.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'The originally planned date for the design review event.']
  sign_off_authority varchar [note: 'Name or role of the individual or body with authority to approve or reject the design at this review stage (e.g., Chief Engineer, Client Representative, Regulatory Authority).']
  sign_off_date date [note: 'Date on which the sign-off authority formally approved or rejected the design package.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system from which this design review record originated (e.g., Aconex, BIM 360, Procore).']
  stage varchar [note: 'Design completion stage at which the review is conducted. IFC = Issued for Construction; IFD = Issued for Design; stages represent percentage completion milestones. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: concept|30_percent|60_percent|90_percent|ifc|ifd|as_built — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  summary varchar [note: 'Executive summary or key findings from the design review event.']
  total_comments_raised varchar [note: 'Total number of review comments or issues raised during this design review event.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for each formal design review event including internal peer reviews, interdisciplinary checks (IDC), client milestone reviews, and third-party/authority reviews. Captures review type, review date, attendees, design package under review, review stage (30%/60%/90%/IFC), disposition (accepted, conditionally accepted, rejected), and sign-off authority. Contains individual review comments as child records with sequential comment number, category (major/minor/informational), comment text, marked-up reference location, design team response, disposition (accepted, rejected, partially accepted), action owner, and closure status. SSOT for all design review and comment resolution data, supporting design approval workflows, client milestone gate management, and regulatory submission readiness.'
}

Business justification: Change notices in construction are formally raised against or directed to the client organisation for approval and cost/schedule impact agreement.   material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to material.material_master. Business justification: Change notices often affect material selections; linking to material_master records the impacted material.']
  originator_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.contact. Business justification: Change notices are originated by a client contact; linking enables change log reporting and responsibility tracking.']

  actual_cost_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual realized financial impact of the change notice after implementation, expressed in the project base currency.']
  actual_schedule_impact_days varchar [note: 'Actual realized schedule impact in calendar days after implementation of the change notice.']
  affected_disciplines varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of all engineering disciplines impacted by this change notice, enabling cross-functional coordination.']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name or role of the individual or committee with authority to approve or reject this change notice (e.g., Project Manager, Chief Engineer, Change Control Board).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the change notice was formally approved by the designated approval authority.']
  bim_model_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the BIM model file or version that incorporates or is affected by this change notice.']

  change_notice_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique business identifier for this change notice, typically following a project-specific numbering convention (e.g., ECN-2024-001).']
  change_notice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the change notice in the approval and implementation workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|rejected|implemented|closed|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  change_notice_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the change notice by its nature (design change, engineering change, specification change, material substitution, scope change, regulatory change).']
  clash_detection_impact varchar [note: 'Description of any clash detection issues identified or resolved as a result of this change notice in the BIM coordination process.']
  client_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the client formally approved this change notice, if client approval was required.']
  client_approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether formal client approval is contractually required for this change notice before implementation.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional notes, remarks, or commentary related to the change notice, including review feedback and coordination notes.']
  cost_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this change notice has a financial cost impact on the project budget.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this change notice record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for cost impact amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  date_raised date [note: 'Date on which the change notice was formally raised or submitted into the change management system.']
  discipline varchar [note: 'Primary engineering discipline impacted by this change notice (e.g., civil, structural, mechanical, electrical, plumbing, architectural).']
  estimated_cost_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated financial impact of the change notice on project cost, expressed in the project base currency. Positive values indicate cost increases; negative values indicate savings.']
  estimated_schedule_impact_days varchar [note: 'Estimated number of calendar days by which the project schedule will be extended or accelerated due to this change notice. Positive values indicate delays; negative values indicate acceleration.']
  implementation_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which implementation of the change notice was fully completed and verified.']
  implementation_start_date date [note: 'Date on which implementation of the approved change notice commenced on site or in design documentation.']
  implementation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the physical implementation of the approved change notice in the field or design deliverables.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this change notice record was last updated or modified in the system.']
  originating_cause varchar [note: 'Root cause or trigger that necessitated the change notice (client instruction, unforeseen site condition, design error, regulatory requirement, value engineering, constructability improvement).']
  originator_organization varchar [note: 'Organization or company of the individual who initiated the change notice (e.g., client, contractor, subcontractor, consultant).']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level indicating urgency and impact of the change notice on project schedule and operations.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this change notice is driven by or impacts regulatory compliance requirements (building codes, safety standards, environmental regulations).']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Citation of the specific regulatory code, standard, or requirement that necessitates or is impacted by this change notice.']
  rejection_date date [note: 'Date on which the change notice was formally rejected by the approval authority, if applicable.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the change notice was rejected, including technical, commercial, or procedural justifications.']
  schedule_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this change notice has an impact on the project schedule or critical path.']
  title varchar [note: 'Brief descriptive title summarizing the nature of the design or engineering change.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each formal design change or engineering change notice (ECN) issued during the project. Tracks change number, originating cause (client instruction, site condition, regulatory requirement, value engineering), affected drawings and specifications, design disciplines impacted, cost and schedule impact assessment, approval authority, and implementation status. Feeds into the CO (Change Order) process.'
}

Table procurement.vendor {
  vendor_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vendor record. Primary key for the vendor master data product. System-generated surrogate key used across all procurement and supply chain transactions.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'SAP vendor account number assigned in SAP MM Vendor Master. External business identifier used in purchase orders, invoices, and payment processing. Unique across the ERP system.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'First line of the vendor\'s registered business address. Typically contains street number and street name. Used for correspondence, legal notices, and payment remittance.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the vendor\'s business address. Contains suite, floor, building, or other secondary address information. Nullable if not applicable.']
  annual_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Vendor\'s reported annual revenue in the vendor\'s default currency. Used for vendor size classification, capacity assessment, and financial stability evaluation. Typically self-reported or obtained from financial statements.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor was approved for procurement transactions following successful completion of prequalification, due diligence, and compliance checks. Nullable for prospective vendors not yet approved.']
  audit_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the most recent vendor audit. Passed indicates full compliance; passed with conditions indicates minor findings requiring corrective action; failed indicates major non-conformances; not audited indicates no audit has been conducted.']
  bank_account_number varchar [note: 'Vendor\'s bank account number for electronic payment receipt. Highly sensitive financial information used for ACH and wire transfer processing. Encrypted at rest and in transit.']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'Name of the financial institution where the vendor maintains their primary business account for payment receipt. Used for electronic payment processing and wire transfers.']
  bank_routing_number varchar [note: 'Bank routing number, SWIFT code, or IBAN for the vendor\'s bank account. Used to route electronic payments to the correct financial institution. Format varies by country and payment network.']
  bonding_capacity_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum bonding capacity available to the vendor in the vendor\'s default currency. Represents the total value of performance bonds and payment bonds the vendor can secure from their surety. Critical for subcontractor prequalification and large contract awards.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the vendor\'s registered business address is located. Used for geographic analysis, local sourcing initiatives, and logistics planning.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Primary business classification of the vendor based on the type of goods or services provided. Determines procurement workflows, approval hierarchies, and contract templates. Material suppliers provide construction materials; equipment rental provides machinery; specialist subcontractors perform MEP, civil, or structural work; general contractors manage construction execution; professional services include design, engineering, and consulting; utility providers supply power, water, and telecommunications.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the vendor\'s registered business location. Used for international trade compliance, currency determination, and cross-border procurement regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vendor record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and vendor relationship tenure analysis. System-generated and immutable.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'Credit rating assigned by credit rating agencies (Dun & Bradstreet, Moody\'s, S&P) reflecting the vendor\'s financial stability and creditworthiness. Used for vendor risk assessment and payment term determination. NR indicates not rated. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: aaa|aa|a|bbb|bb|b|ccc|cc|c|d|nr — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the vendor\'s default transaction currency. Determines the currency used for purchase orders, invoices, and payments. Used for foreign exchange exposure analysis.']
  diversity_classification varchar [note: 'Diversity certification status for the vendor. MBE (Minority Business Enterprise), WBE (Women Business Enterprise), DBE (Disadvantaged Business Enterprise), SDVOSB (Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Small Business), HUBZone (Historically Underutilized Business Zone). Used for diversity spend reporting and compliance with client diversity requirements.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Nine-digit DUNS number issued by Dun & Bradstreet for unique identification of business entities. Used for credit checks, vendor risk assessment, and compliance with government contracting requirements.']
  employee_count varchar [note: 'Total number of employees working for the vendor organization. Used for vendor capacity assessment, small business classification, and resource availability evaluation.']
  geographic_coverage varchar [note: 'Geographic regions or markets where the vendor operates and can provide services. Free-text field listing countries, states, or regions. Used for project-specific vendor selection and logistics planning.']
  insurance_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Certificate number for the vendor\'s current general liability and professional indemnity insurance policy. Used to verify insurance coverage before contract execution and during vendor compliance audits.']
  insurance_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the vendor\'s current insurance certificate. Monitored to ensure continuous insurance coverage throughout the contract period. Triggers renewal reminders and compliance alerts.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent vendor audit or compliance review. Audits assess quality systems, safety practices, financial controls, and contract compliance. Used to schedule periodic re-audits and maintain vendor qualification status.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vendor record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change to any field in the vendor master record. Used for change tracking, data quality monitoring, and synchronization with downstream systems.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Preferred payment method for remitting payment to the vendor. ACH for automated clearing house electronic transfer; wire transfer for same-day international payments; check for traditional paper payments; credit card for small purchases; letter of credit for international trade.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms code defining the payment schedule and discount structure for this vendor. Examples include NET30 (net 30 days), NET60, 2/10NET30 (2% discount if paid within 10 days, otherwise net 30). Drives accounts payable processing and cash flow planning.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the vendor\'s business address. Used for mail delivery, logistics planning, and geographic segmentation. Format varies by country.']
