  inspection_requirements varchar [note: 'Description of quality inspection and testing requirements for the procured materials or equipment (e.g., FAT, SAT, third-party certification, mill test certificates).']
  is_critical_path boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the materials or services covered by this plan are on the project critical path and require expedited procurement to avoid schedule delays.']
  is_long_lead_item boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this sourcing plan covers long-lead items requiring early procurement action due to extended manufacturing or delivery lead times.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sourcing plan record was last updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, assumptions, constraints, or special instructions related to this sourcing plan.']
  packaging_strategy varchar [note: 'Description of how procurement scope is bundled or split into packages for tendering (e.g., single package, split by trade, split by project phase, geographic split).']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms to be applied for this procurement (e.g., Net 30, progress payments, milestone-based, letter of credit).']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the sourcing plan. Externally visible reference number used in procurement documentation and communications.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the sourcing plan indicating its approval state and execution readiness.']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the sourcing plan. Incremented when the plan is revised to track changes over the project lifecycle.']
  planned_award_date date [note: 'Target date for awarding the purchase order or contract to the selected supplier. Critical milestone for procurement lead time calculation.']
  planned_bid_closing_date date [note: 'Target date by which vendor bids or proposals are due for evaluation.']
  planned_delivery_date date [note: 'Target date for material or equipment delivery to site. Must align with construction schedule requirements and site readiness.']
  planned_installation_date date [note: 'Target date for installation or commissioning of the procured materials or equipment. Drives backward scheduling of procurement activities.']
  planned_rfq_date date [note: 'Target date for issuing the RFQ or RFP to potential suppliers. Aligns with project procurement schedule milestones.']
  preferred_supplier_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of preferred or prequalified supplier names or codes to be invited for this procurement.']
  procurement_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the procurement scope covered by this plan (e.g., Structural Steel, MEP Equipment, Concrete Materials, Construction Services). Aligns with project WBS and material classification hierarchy.']
  procurement_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Total lead time in days from purchase order award to site delivery, including manufacturing, inspection, and logistics time.']
  retention_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of payment to be retained until completion or end of defects liability period. Typical range 5-10%.']
  risk_classification varchar [note: 'Risk level associated with this procurement based on factors such as value, complexity, lead time, supplier market, and schedule impact.']
  sourcing_method varchar [note: 'Procurement strategy and method to be used for acquiring the materials or services (e.g., competitive bidding, sole source negotiation, call-off from framework agreement).']
  special_requirements varchar [note: 'Any special procurement requirements such as local content mandates, sustainability certifications (LEED), HSE requirements, or regulatory compliance needs.']
  supplier_prequalification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether suppliers must be prequalified before being invited to bid for this procurement.']
  warranty_period_months varchar [note: 'Required warranty or defects liability period in months for the procured materials or equipment.']

  Note: 'Project-level procurement execution plan defining the sourcing strategy, procurement milestones, long-lead item identification, packaging strategy, expediting requirements, and planned award dates for all major material and service categories. Captures plan version, project reference, procurement category, sourcing method (competitive bid, sole source, framework agreement), planned RFQ date, planned award date, planned delivery date, expediting frequency and method, responsible procurement manager, and critical path material flags. Integrates with Primavera P6 schedule milestones to ensure procurement activities align with construction sequencing.'
}

Table procurement.vendor_evaluation {
  vendor_evaluation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for vendor_evaluation']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project for which this vendor evaluation was conducted. Applicable for project-specific evaluations tied to bid eligibility.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Vendor performance evaluations in construction measure compliance with specific regulatory obligations (HSE regulations, environmental standards). Linking vendor_evaluation to regulatory_obligation en']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor being evaluated. Links to the vendor master record in the procurement system.']
  work_front_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.work_front. Business justification: Work-front-level vendor performance evaluation: vendor evaluations in construction are conducted for specific work fronts where the vendor performed (e.g., earthworks front, concrete front). This link']
  avl_entry_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor was added to the AVL (Approved Vendor List) following successful pre-qualification. Null for vendors not yet approved.']
  bid_invitation_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) determining whether the vendor is currently eligible to receive bid invitations for RFP (Request for Proposal) and RFQ (Request for Quotation) based on evaluation outcome and qualification status.']
  bonding_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum contract value (in USD) for which the vendor can provide performance and payment bonds. Critical for pre-qualification and determines eligibility for large-scale EPC (Engineering Procurement and Construction) projects.']
  commercial_compliance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score (0-100) evaluating the vendor\'s adherence to contract terms, invoicing accuracy, payment terms compliance, and commercial documentation completeness.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of corrective actions required from the vendor to address performance gaps, quality issues, HSE violations, or compliance deficiencies identified during evaluation.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the vendor must complete and demonstrate closure of required corrective actions. Failure to meet this date may result in suspension or disqualification.']
  corrective_action_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) signaling that the vendor must implement corrective actions to address identified deficiencies before qualification can be maintained or upgraded.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vendor evaluation record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record lifecycle tracking.']
  evaluation_date date [note: 'Date when the evaluation was formally completed and scored. Represents the business event timestamp for the assessment.']
  evaluation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing additional observations, strengths, weaknesses, and contextual information from the evaluator that supplements the quantitative scores.']
  evaluation_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the vendor evaluation record. Format: VE-YYYYMMDD followed by sequence number.']
  evaluation_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the performance evaluation period. For periodic reviews, defines the close of the assessment window.']
  evaluation_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the performance evaluation period. For periodic reviews, defines the beginning of the assessment window. For pre-qualification, represents the date evaluation commenced.']
  evaluation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the vendor evaluation. Tracks progression from draft through approval or rejection.']
  evaluation_type varchar [note: 'Type of vendor evaluation being conducted. Pre-qualification for new vendors entering AVL (Approved Vendor List), periodic review for ongoing performance assessment, project-specific for bid eligibility, re-qualification for expired certifications, or incident-triggered for corrective action follow-up.']
  financial_health_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score (0-100) assessing the vendor\'s financial stability based on credit rating, liquidity ratios, debt-to-equity, and financial statement analysis. Critical for pre-qualification and bonding capacity assessment.']
  hse_incident_count varchar [note: 'Total number of HSE (Health Safety and Environment) incidents (including LTI, near-misses, and safety violations) involving the vendor during the evaluation period.']
  hse_rating_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score (0-100) assessing the vendor\'s HSE (Health Safety and Environment) performance including TRIR (Total Recordable Incident Rate), LTI (Lost Time Injury) frequency, safety certifications (ISO 45001), and HSE management system maturity.']
  insurance_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) confirming that the vendor\'s general liability, professional indemnity, and workers\' compensation insurance policies have been verified and meet project requirements.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vendor evaluation record was last updated. Audit trail for change tracking and data freshness monitoring.']
  ncr_count varchar [note: 'Total number of NCR (Non-Conformance Report) incidents raised against the vendor during the evaluation period. Lower counts indicate better quality performance.']
  on_time_delivery_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage (0-100) of deliveries completed on or before the scheduled delivery date during the evaluation period. Key KPI for schedule performance and reliability.']
  overall_kpi_rating decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite numerical score (0-100) representing the weighted average of all evaluation dimensions (financial, technical, HSE, quality, delivery, responsiveness, commercial). Determines overall vendor performance grade.']
  performance_grade varchar [note: 'Letter grade (A through F) assigned based on overall KPI rating. A=Excellent (90-100), B=Good (80-89), C=Satisfactory (70-79), D=Marginal (60-69), F=Unsatisfactory (<60). Drives vendor tier classification and bid invitation priority.']
  qualification_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor\'s qualification status expires and requires renewal through re-evaluation. Typically set 12-24 months from evaluation date for qualified vendors.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Resulting qualification status of the vendor based on evaluation outcome. Determines vendor eligibility for bid invitations and PO (Purchase Order) issuance.']
  quality_acceptance_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage (0-100) of delivered materials or services that passed quality inspection on first submission without NCR (Non-Conformance Report) during the evaluation period.']
  quality_certification_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score (0-100) evaluating the vendor\'s quality management certifications (ISO 9001), QA/QC (Quality Assurance/Quality Control) processes, inspection capabilities, and adherence to ITP (Inspection and Test Plan) requirements.']
  responsiveness_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score (0-100) assessing the vendor\'s responsiveness to RFI (Request for Information), RFQ (Request for Quotation), change orders, and issue resolution. Measures communication effectiveness and turnaround time.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system from which the evaluation data was sourced. Procore for project-specific evaluations, SAP MM for vendor master and procurement data, Intelex for HSE performance data, or manual for offline assessments.']
  technical_capability_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score (0-100) evaluating the vendor\'s technical competence, equipment availability, workforce skills, past project experience, and ability to meet technical specifications.']

  Note: 'Unified vendor assessment records covering both pre-qualification (AVL entry, financial health, technical capability, HSE ratings, quality certifications, bonding limits, insurance verification) and ongoing periodic performance evaluation (on-time delivery rate, quality acceptance rate, NCR count, HSE incident count, responsiveness score, commercial compliance score, overall KPI rating). Captures qualification status, evaluation type (pre-qualification, periodic review, project-specific), evaluation period, evaluating manager, corrective action flags, and qualification expiry dates. Feeds vendor approval decisions, bid invitation eligibility, and continuous improvement programs. Sourced from Procore, SAP MM, and Intelex.'
}

  wbs_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to schedule.wbs_node. Business justification: Purchase requisitions carry a plain-text wbs_element. A FK to wbs_node enables WBS-based procurement commitment tracking and budget availability checks — a core project controls function. wbs_element ']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the purchase requisition received final approval and became eligible for conversion to RFQ or PO.']
  budget_available_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether sufficient budget is available in the assigned WBS element or cost center to cover the estimated cost of this requisition. True if budget is available, False if insufficient.']
  budget_variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between available budget and the estimated total cost. Positive value indicates surplus budget, negative indicates deficit requiring approval override.']
  closed_date date [note: 'Date when the purchase requisition was administratively closed after full conversion to procurement documents or cancellation, marking the end of its active lifecycle.']
  conversion_date date [note: 'Date when the purchase requisition was converted to an RFQ or PO document.']
  conversion_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether and how the approved purchase requisition has been converted into downstream procurement documents (RFQ for competitive bidding or direct PO for known vendors).']
  converted_document_number varchar [note: 'Document number of the RFQ or PO that was created from this purchase requisition, establishing traceability through the procurement lifecycle.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center to which the requisition cost is allocated, used when the requisition is not project-specific or for indirect procurement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the purchase requisition record was first created in the ERP system. Used for audit trail and process cycle time analysis.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated cost amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, AED).']
  current_approver_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual currently responsible for reviewing and approving the purchase requisition in the workflow sequence.']
  delivery_location varchar [note: 'Site address, warehouse, or specific location where the materials or equipment should be delivered. Critical for logistics planning and site coordination.']
  estimated_total_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total estimated cost for the requisition line, calculated as quantity multiplied by estimated unit cost. Used for budget availability verification.']
  estimated_unit_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated cost per unit of the requested material or service, used for budget checking and preliminary cost estimation.']
  justification_notes varchar [note: 'Business justification and rationale for the purchase requisition, including project need, schedule impact, and any special circumstances requiring expedited processing.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the purchase requisition record was last updated, capturing any changes to status, approvals, or requisition details.']
  material_group varchar [note: 'Classification code grouping similar materials or services for procurement strategy, vendor assignment, and reporting (e.g., concrete, steel, electrical, MEP services).']
  pr_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the purchase requisition, externally visible and used in procurement workflows and communications.']
  pr_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the purchase requisition in the approval and conversion workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|rejected|converted|cancelled|closed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  pr_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the purchase requisition based on procurement category: standard materials, subcontract services, general services, stock transfers between sites, consignment arrangements, or equipment rental.']
  preferred_vendor_code varchar [note: 'Vendor code of the preferred or suggested supplier for this requisition, if the requester has a specific vendor recommendation based on prior performance or technical requirements.']
  procurement_strategy varchar [note: 'Sourcing approach to be used for fulfilling this requisition based on value, complexity, vendor availability, and time constraints.']
  quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of the material or service being requested, expressed in the unit of measure specified.']
  rejection_date date [note: 'Date when the purchase requisition was rejected by an approver in the workflow, if applicable.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided by the approver for rejecting the purchase requisition, including corrective actions required for resubmission.']
  requester_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the requester for communication regarding the purchase requisition status, clarifications, and approvals.']
  requester_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who created and submitted the purchase requisition.']
  requesting_department varchar [note: 'Name or code of the department or functional unit that originated the purchase requisition (e.g., Site Operations, Engineering, Procurement, MEP).']
  required_delivery_date date [note: 'Date by which the requested materials, equipment, or services must be delivered to the project site or requesting location to meet project schedule requirements.']
  requisition_date date [note: 'Date when the purchase requisition was created and submitted by the requesting party. Principal business event timestamp for the requisition.']
  technical_specification_attached boolean [note: 'Indicates whether detailed technical specifications, drawings, or engineering documents are attached to support the requisition evaluation and vendor quotation process.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit in which the requested quantity is measured (e.g., EA for each, M for meter, M3 for cubic meter, KG for kilogram, HR for hour). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|M|M2|M3|KG|TON|L|HR|DAY|SET|LOT — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  urgency_classification varchar [note: 'Priority level of the purchase requisition based on project schedule impact and delivery timeline requirements. Emergency requisitions bypass standard approval workflows.']

  Note: 'Internal purchase requisition raised by project or site teams to initiate procurement of materials, equipment, or services. Captures requisition number, requesting department, project and WBS element, material or service description, required quantity, unit of measure, required delivery date, estimated cost, budget availability flag, approval workflow status (pending, approved, rejected), approver, urgency classification, and conversion status (converted to RFQ or PO). Sourced from SAP MM PR process. Entry point for all formal procurement activity.'
}

Table procurement.vendor_invoice {
  vendor_invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vendor invoice record. Primary key.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Ensures invoice verification against contract terms, supporting audit of payments versus agreed contract values.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.asset. Business justification: Invoices for equipment purchases/rentals must link to the asset to close the financial‑asset loop for accounting and warranty tracking.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.account. Business justification: Vendor invoices are billed to a specific client account; FK supports payment processing and client‑level invoice reconciliation.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project to which this invoice is allocated. Used for project cost tracking and job costing.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cost center to which this invoice is charged for financial accounting and cost control purposes.']
  finance_cost_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_code. Business justification: Vendor invoices need a cost‑code reference to map spend to project cost structures and support earned‑value analysis.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the general ledger account to which this invoice is posted in the financial accounting system.']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.goods_receipt. Business justification: Three-way match (PO + Goods Receipt + Invoice) is a foundational procurement control. vendor_invoice already references purchase_order_id; adding goods_receipt_id completes the three-way match chain w']
  payment_certificate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.payment_certificate. Business justification: In construction, a payment certificate authorizes the vendor invoice for payment. Linking vendor_invoice to payment_certificate supports 3-way match validation, accounts payable reconciliation, and ce']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order against which this invoice was received. Used for three-way match validation (PO-GR-Invoice).']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor who issued this invoice. Links to vendor master data.']
  work_front_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.work_front. Business justification: Subcontractor invoice allocation to work fronts: vendor invoices for subcontracted work must be allocated to specific work fronts for cost-code reporting, earned value management, and budget variance ']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the invoice was approved for payment by the authorized approver.']
  blocked_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the invoice is blocked from payment, such as pending approval, missing documentation, price variance, or quality hold.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this invoice record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code indicating the currency in which the invoice is denominated.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total discount amount applied to the invoice, including early payment discounts, volume discounts, or negotiated rebates.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this invoice is currently under dispute due to discrepancies in quantity, quality, pricing, or terms.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the reason for the invoice dispute, such as quantity mismatch, pricing error, quality issues, or missing documentation.']
  fi_document_number varchar [note: 'The SAP FI document number generated when the invoice is posted to the financial accounting system. This is the system-generated accounting document reference.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year in which this invoice is recorded.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which this invoice is recorded for financial reporting purposes.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'The date the vendor issued the invoice. This is the principal business event timestamp for the invoice transaction.']
  invoice_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the goods or services covered by this invoice, providing context for the transaction.']
  invoice_gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total invoice amount before taxes and adjustments. Represents the base value of goods or services invoiced.']
  invoice_net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total invoice amount payable after applying taxes, discounts, and adjustments. This is the final amount due to the vendor.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'The externally-issued invoice number provided by the vendor. This is the vendor\'s unique identifier for the invoice document.']
  invoice_received_date date [note: 'The date the invoice was received by the procurement or accounts payable department.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the vendor invoice in the verification and payment workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|received|under_review|blocked|approved|posted|paid|disputed|cancelled — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  invoice_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the invoice document type indicating the nature of the transaction (standard invoice, credit memo for returns, debit memo for additional charges, etc.). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|credit_memo|debit_memo|prepayment|final|progress|retention_release — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this invoice record was last modified or updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments related to this invoice, such as special instructions, clarifications, or internal remarks.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The actual date on which payment was made to the vendor for this invoice.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'The date by which payment must be made to the vendor to comply with payment terms and avoid late payment penalties.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The method by which payment will be made to the vendor for this invoice.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the payment transaction when the invoice is paid, linking the invoice to the payment record.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'The agreed payment terms for this invoice, such as Net 30, Net 60, or 2/10 Net 30, defining the payment schedule and any early payment discount conditions.']
  retention_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount withheld from the invoice payment as retention per contract terms, typically released upon project completion or after the Defects Liability Period (DLP).']
  retention_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the invoice amount withheld as retention, typically ranging from 5% to 10% per construction contract terms.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount applied to the invoice, including sales tax, VAT, GST, or other applicable taxes.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'The tax code applied to this invoice, defining the tax rate and tax type (VAT, GST, sales tax) per jurisdiction.']
  three_way_match_status varchar [note: 'Result of the three-way match validation comparing purchase order, goods receipt (GR), and invoice. Indicates whether quantities, prices, and terms align across all three documents.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of the invoice verification process indicating whether the invoice has been validated against the purchase order and goods receipt.']
  withholding_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of withholding tax deducted from the invoice payment per tax regulations, to be remitted to tax authorities.']

  Note: 'Vendor invoices received against purchase orders capturing invoice number, vendor, PO reference, invoice date, invoice amount, tax amount, currency, payment due date, three-way match status (PO-GR-Invoice), invoice verification status (blocked, approved, posted), SAP FI document number, and dispute flag. Distinct from client billing invoices owned by the finance domain — this is the SSOT for vendor payable documents in procurement.'
}

Table procurement.inspection_release {
  inspection_release_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inspection release record. Primary key for vendor quality compliance and documentation tracking.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project for which this inspection is being performed. Links inspection to project context.']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.goods_receipt. Business justification: An inspection release (FAT, pre-shipment inspection, site inspection) is performed on physically received goods. Linking inspection_release to the specific goods_receipt record enables quality traceab']
  itp_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.itp. Business justification: Material inspection releases are conducted per the governing ITP. Construction QA requires tracing each inspection release to the ITP that defines acceptance criteria and hold/witness points. \'itp_ref']
  material_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.material_catalog. Business justification: Link inspection release material to master catalog for normalization and remove redundant material_id column.']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_permit. Business justification: Material and equipment inspection releases in construction must reference the compliance permit authorizing the work or import (e.g., hazardous material handling permits, environmental permits). QA/co']
  po_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.po_line. Business justification: An inspection release is performed at the PO line level — each line item may have distinct inspection requirements (quality_inspection_required on material_catalog). inspection_release already has pur']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order under which this inspection is performed. Links inspection to procurement contract.']
  schedule_milestone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to schedule.schedule_milestone. Business justification: Inspection releases gate milestone completion on construction projects (e.g., \'All Structural Steel Inspected and Released\' milestone). QA managers and schedulers jointly track inspection release stat']
  specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to material.specification. Business justification: Inspection releases in construction verify materials against engineering specifications (ITP process). inspection_release.technical_specification_reference (plain text) is a denormalized reference to ']
  technical_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.technical_specification. Business justification: Vendor inspection and release of materials is conducted against technical specifications (ITP process). The existing plain-text \'technical_specification_reference\' column is a denormalization of desig']
  test_certificate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.test_certificate. Business justification: Material inspection release (MRR/IRN) in construction requires referencing the quality test certificate submitted by the vendor. \'test_report_document_number\' and \'mill_certificate_document_number\' ar']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor whose materials or equipment are being inspected. Links to vendor master data.']
  aconex_transmittal_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number for the Aconex document transmittal through which the inspection documents were formally submitted and distributed.']
  as_built_drawing_document_number varchar [note: 'Document control number for as-built drawings submitted by vendor showing final fabricated dimensions and configurations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inspection release record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  document_review_date date [note: 'Date when the document review was completed and approval status was assigned.']
  document_review_status varchar [note: 'Current review status of the vendor-submitted inspection and technical documents. Tracks document approval workflow.']
  document_reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the engineer or QA/QC specialist who reviewed and approved the inspection documentation.']
  document_revision_number varchar [note: 'Revision identifier for the inspection documentation package. Tracks version control for resubmissions and updates.']
  document_submission_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor submitted the inspection documentation package for review.']
  equipment_tag_number varchar [note: 'Unique tag identifier for the specific equipment unit being inspected. Used for traceability in MEP and major equipment installations.']
  inspection_date date [note: 'Date when the physical inspection or test was conducted. Principal business event timestamp for the inspection activity.']
  inspection_location varchar [note: 'Physical location where the inspection was performed (e.g., vendor factory, fabrication yard, construction site, third-party testing facility).']
  inspection_order_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the inspection order issued to vendor or third-party inspector. Format: IO-YYYYNNNN.']
  inspection_remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text field for inspector comments, observations, special conditions, or additional notes regarding the inspection findings.']
  inspection_report_document_number varchar [note: 'Document control number for the formal inspection report. Links to document management system for full report retrieval.']
  inspection_result varchar [note: 'Overall outcome of the inspection. Pass indicates full compliance, conditional pass indicates minor non-conformances with agreed remediation, fail indicates rejection, pending retest indicates awaiting re-inspection.']
  inspection_scope varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the inspection scope, including specific tests performed, standards applied, and acceptance criteria used.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inspection order. Tracks progression from scheduling through completion and release.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Category of inspection performed. FAT (Factory Acceptance Test), SAT (Site Acceptance Test), pre-shipment inspection, third-party inspection, dimensional check, witness test, mill inspection, hydrostatic test, or non-destructive test (NDT). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FAT|SAT|pre_shipment|third_party|dimensional_check|witness_test|mill_inspection|hydrostatic_test|non_destructive_test — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  inspection_witness_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether client or third-party witness is contractually required to be present during inspection. True if witness hold point is specified in ITP.']
  inspector_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the inspector or inspection agency representative who performed the inspection. May be internal QA/QC staff, third-party inspector, or client representative.']
  inspector_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or agency that performed the inspection (e.g., internal QA/QC team, third-party inspection agency, client representative).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inspection release record was last updated. Audit trail for record modifications.']
  material_data_sheet_document_number varchar [note: 'Document control number for the vendor-submitted material data sheet. Contains material properties, composition, and technical characteristics.']
  ncr_count varchar [note: 'Number of Non-Conformance Reports raised during this inspection. Zero indicates full compliance with specifications.']
  om_manual_document_number varchar [note: 'Document control number for the vendor-submitted Operations and Maintenance manual. Required for equipment handover and commissioning.']
  punch_list_items varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or detailed description of outstanding punch list items, deficiencies, or minor non-conformances identified during inspection that require remediation before final release.']
  release_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Unique certificate number issued upon successful inspection, authorizing shipment or installation. Format: RC-YYYYNNNN. Required before goods can be shipped to site.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date when the inspection release certificate was issued and materials were authorized for shipment or installation.']
  shipment_authorization_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor was formally authorized to ship the inspected materials to the construction site following successful inspection release.']
  witness_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the client organization or third-party entity that witnessed the inspection, if witness was required.']
  witness_representative_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual representative from the witness organization who attended the inspection.']

  Note: 'Vendor quality compliance and documentation records encompassing Factory Acceptance Tests (FAT), pre-shipment inspections, and all vendor-submitted technical documents/submittals required under a PO or framework agreement. Captures inspection order number, vendor, material/equipment tag, inspection type (FAT, SAT, third-party inspection, dimensional check), inspection date, inspector name, inspection result (pass, conditional pass, fail), punch list items, release certificate number, and associated vendor documents (material data sheets, mill certificates, test reports, ITP submissions, O&M manuals, as-built drawings) with document number, revision, submission date, review status (under review, approved, approved with comments, rejected), document type, and Aconex transmittal reference. Required before goods are shipped to site. SSOT for vendor quality evidence and technical submittals in procurement.'
}

Business justification: Construction services (waste disposal, environmental monitoring, asbestos removal) are governed by specific regulatory obligations. The service catalog has regulatory_approval_status and compliance_re']
  skill_trade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.skill_trade. Business justification: Construction services (scaffolding, welding, concrete pumping) map directly to specific skill trades. Procurement teams use this link to match service catalog entries to workforce trade requirements f']

  Note: 'Master reference table for service. Referenced by service_id.'

Table material.master {
  master_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for master']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: In construction ERP systems (SAP, Oracle), material master records carry a valuation GL account for inventory accounting. This account drives automatic posting of inventory movements. A construction f']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Hazardous/regulated material masters (COSHH, REACH, EPA) are governed by specific regulatory obligations. Compliance officers need to identify all materials subject to a given obligation for procureme']
  substitution_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of an approved substitute material.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary supplier for this material.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the material record was approved for use.']
  conformance_certificate_date date [note: 'Date the compliance certificate was issued.']
  conformance_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the material’s compliance certificate (e.g., ISO, ASTM).']
  cost_per_unit decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard cost of one unit of material in the specified currency.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the material master record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code for cost fields.']
  dimensions_cm varchar [note: 'Length×Width×Height dimensions in centimeters, formatted as LxWxH.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the material batch expires.']
  hazardous_material_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the material is classified as hazardous under OSHA/EPA regulations.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Average number of days from order to receipt.']
  leeds_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material is eligible for LEED credit consideration.']
  leeds_credit_applicability varchar [note: 'LEED credit categories the material can contribute to.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the material record.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier used for quality control and recall management.']
  manufacturer_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 code of the manufacturer’s country.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the company that manufactures the material.']
  master_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the material, including typical applications.']
  master_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the material.']
  material_code varchar [note: 'Enterprise‑wide unique code or catalogue number for the material.']
  material_origin_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code of the material\'s source.']
  max_order_qty varchar [note: 'Largest quantity that can be ordered in a single purchase.']
  min_order_qty varchar [note: 'Smallest quantity that can be ordered from the supplier.']
  minimum_performance_criteria varchar [note: 'Minimum performance thresholds the material must meet (e.g., compressive strength).']
  reorder_point_qty varchar [note: 'Inventory level that triggers a replenishment order.']
  safety_stock_qty varchar [note: 'Buffer stock kept to protect against demand variability.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the material can be stored before it expires.']
  specification_grade varchar [note: 'Grade or class of the material as defined by the governing standard.']
  specification_standard varchar [note: 'Reference standard governing the material (e.g., ASTM, BS, ISO, ACI, AISC).']
  storage_requirements varchar [note: 'Special storage conditions (e.g., temperature, humidity, segregation).']
  substitution_approval_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the client/engineer approval document for the substitution.']
  substitution_reason varchar [note: 'Engineering or client‑approved justification for the substitution.']
  supplier_part_number varchar [note: 'Supplier‑assigned part number for the material.']
  technical_specification varchar [note: 'Full technical specification document reference or summary.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit used for inventory and consumption tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the material record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the material record for change control.']
  volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Physical volume of a single unit of material in cubic meters.']
  weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Net weight of a single unit of material in kilograms.']

  Note: 'Authoritative master record for all construction materials and consumables used across projects. Captures material identity, detailed technical specifications (applicable standards — ASTM, BS, ISO, ACI, AISC — grade/class, minimum performance criteria, specification code, specification revision), classification (concrete, steel, MEP components, aggregates, timber, formwork, etc.), unit of measure, approved equivalent substitutes with engineering justification, LEED compliance flags, LEED credit applicability, hazardous material indicators, shelf life, storage requirements, and supplier cross-references. Manages specification lifecycle (draft, approved, superseded) and material qualification status. Records approved substitutions with reason, client/engineer approval reference, cost impact, and affected BOQ lines. This is the SSOT for material identity, technical specifications, and approved substitutes in the construction enterprise — all transactions reference this record.'
}

Table material.warehouse {
  warehouse_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for warehouse']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.account. Business justification: REQUIRED: Some warehouses are owned or leased by a client; the FK enables asset ownership and liability tracking.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Construction warehouses are assigned to cost centers for inventory carrying cost allocation and overhead reporting. A construction finance controller expects each warehouse to reference a cost center ']
  hse_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.hse_plan. Business justification: Warehouses storing hazardous materials must operate under a governing HSE plan defining storage limits, segregation, emergency procedures, and inspection requirements. The warehouse already tracks haz']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_permit. Business justification: Warehouses storing hazardous materials require active environmental/hazardous storage permits. Regulators and site managers must verify the permit authorizing hazardous storage at each warehouse. haza']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the construction site or project to which the warehouse is attached.']
  access_control_method varchar [note: 'Primary method used to control entry to the warehouse.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the warehouse.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, unit, etc.).']
  capacity_area_sqm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum usable floor area in square metres.']
  capacity_weight_tonnes decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum total weight the warehouse can safely store, expressed in metric tonnes.']
  certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the hazardous‑material certification expires.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the warehouse is located.']
  climate_control_type varchar [note: 'Specific climate control method applied when temperature_controlled is true.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the warehouse location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the warehouse record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the warehouse became operational or was officially opened.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the warehouse ceased operations or is scheduled for closure (null if still active).']
  gps_accuracy_meters varchar [note: 'Estimated positional accuracy of the GPS coordinates, in metres.']
  gps_latitude double [note: 'Latitude coordinate of the warehouse centre point.']
  gps_longitude double [note: 'Longitude coordinate of the warehouse centre point.']
  inventory_management_system varchar [note: 'Name of the downstream system that tracks inventory within this warehouse (e.g., SAP WM, Procore).']
  is_hazardous_material_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the warehouse holds a valid hazardous‑material storage certification.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent safety or compliance inspection.']
  next_inspection_due date [note: 'Planned date for the next required inspection.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form remarks or operational comments.']
  operating_hours varchar [note: 'Standard daily operating window (e.g., 08:00‑17:00).']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code for the warehouse address.']
  security_level varchar [note: 'Overall security classification of the warehouse.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province of the warehouse location.']
  storage_zone_details varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of internal zones, racks, bins, or yard sections.']
  temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'True if the warehouse maintains a controlled temperature environment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the warehouse record.']
  warehouse_code varchar [note: 'Business‑visible alphanumeric code used to reference the warehouse in external systems.']
  warehouse_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the warehouse, its purpose, and special characteristics.']
  warehouse_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the warehouse or laydown yard.']
  warehouse_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the warehouse.']
  warehouse_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the warehouse based on construction and regulatory characteristics.']

  Note: 'Master record for physical warehouse, laydown yard, and storage locations across construction sites and central depots. Tracks warehouse type (bonded, open yard, climate-controlled, hazmat-certified), capacity (area sqm, weight tonnes), GPS coordinates, site association, custodian, operating hours, hazardous material storage certification, and granular storage locations (rack positions, bin rows, open yard zones). Supports multi-site inventory management, material transfer logistics, and FIFO/FEFO stock management for batch-tracked materials.'
}

Table material.stock_level {
  stock_level_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each stock level record.']
  inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection. Business justification: Quality hold management: stock_level.quality_inspection_quantity tracks material on quality hold. Linking to quality.inspection identifies which inspection is clearing the hold, enabling warehouse man']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'FK to material.material_master']
  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'FK to material.warehouse']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the production batch, used for traceability of concrete, steel, etc.']
  blocked_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity held due to quality issues, holds, or regulatory restrictions.']
  committed_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity allocated to specific construction activities or contracts.']
  cost_per_unit decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard cost of a single unit of material in the base currency.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the stock level record was first created.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the material is considered unusable or non‑compliant.']
  in_transit_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity that has been dispatched but not yet received at the location.']
  last_movement_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent stock transaction affecting this record.']
  last_movement_type varchar [note: 'Category of the most recent stock transaction.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the stock level record.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse or storage site where the stock resides.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for a lot of material, often used for quality tracking.']
  max_stock_level decimal(18,3) [note: 'Upper threshold for inventory to avoid over‑stocking.']
  min_stock_level decimal(18,3) [note: 'Lowest permissible inventory level before stock is considered understocked.']
  quality_inspection_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity currently undergoing quality inspection or testing.']
  quantity_on_hand decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total amount of material physically present in the location.']
  reorder_point decimal(18,3) [note: 'Inventory level that triggers a replenishment request.']
  reserved_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity earmarked for pending work packages or purchase orders.']
  safety_stock decimal(18,3) [note: 'Buffer quantity maintained to protect against demand variability or supply delays.']
  stock_level_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the stock quantity.']
  supplier_code varchar [note: 'External identifier of the supplier providing the material.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the quantity fields.']

  Note: 'Current on-hand inventory position for each material at each warehouse/storage location. Captures unrestricted stock, quality-inspection stock, blocked stock, reserved stock, in-transit quantities, and committed (reserved-for-activity) quantities. Tracks reorder point, minimum/maximum stock levels, safety stock thresholds, and last movement date. Updated in real-time by stock movements (receipts, issues, transfers, returns). Critical for BOQ reconciliation, preventing site stoppages due to material shortages, and supporting procurement trigger automation.'
}

Table material.material_boq_line {
  material_boq_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the BOQ line item.']

A construction quantity surveyor expects each BOQ line to reference a cost center along']
  estimate_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to bid.estimate_line.   finance_cost_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_code. Business justification: Required for BOQ Budgeting: each BOQ line is associated with a cost code to track planned vs. actual costs.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material or item that this BOQ line represents.']
  technical_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.technical_specification. Quantity surveyors and cost engineers require this link for cost es']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.wbs_element. Business justification: BOQ lines are directly tied to WBS elements for cost roll-up, EVM, and contract billing. Every construction cost engineer maps BOQ items to WBS nodes. wbs_code is a denormalized representation of wbs_']
  wbs_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to schedule.wbs_node. Business justification: material_boq_line carries a denormalized wbs_code. BOQ lines are structured by WBS in construction cost management. A direct FK to wbs_node supports WBS-level BOQ cost rollup, budget control reporting']
  contract_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the material that is contractually committed in the BOQ.']
  is_change_order boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line originates from a change order (true) or the original BOQ (false).']
  item_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the material or work item represented by the line.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the line within the BOQ, used for sorting and display.']
  material_boq_line_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the BOQ line.']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Planned quantity of the material as defined in the BOQ line, expressed in the unit of measure.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the BOQ line record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the BOQ line record.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the line after each amendment.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source operational system that supplied the BOQ line (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Procore).']
  total_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Calculated line value (contract_quantity × unit_rate) in the project currency.']
  trade_discipline varchar [note: 'Construction trade or discipline (e.g., concrete, steel, MEP) associated with the line.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure for the quantity (e.g., m3, ton, pcs).']
  unit_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'Agreed price per unit of measure for the material.']

  Note: 'Bill of Quantities (BOQ) line item representing material and work quantities baselined for a project, used as the material domain\'s reference for take-off reconciliation, consumption tracking, and procurement planning. Captures BOQ item code, description, unit of measure, contract quantity, unit rate, total value, WBS element, trade/discipline, and revision history. While contractual BOQ ownership may reside in the contract domain, this product serves as the material domain\'s operational view for MTO reconciliation, material consumption variance analysis, and progress measurement against planned quantities.'
}

Construction procurement teams must confirm a risk assessment exis']
  scope_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_scope. Business justification: Scope-to-procurement reconciliation: MTO lines are derived directly from contracted scope of work. Linking mto_line to contract_scope enables quantity surveyors to validate that material take-offs ali']
  technical_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.technical_specification. Business justification: MTO lines must reference the governing technical specification to ensure correct material grade and standard is procured. QA and procurement compliance processes require this traceability. \'material_s']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.wbs_element. Business justification: Material Take-Off lines are generated per WBS element to drive procurement planning and cost-to-complete analysis. Procurement planners and project controls engineers require WBS-level MTO visibility ']
  wbs_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to schedule.wbs_node. Business justification: mto_line carries a denormalized wbs_code. WBS-level material quantity and cost tracking is a standard construction cost management report. Linking MTO lines to wbs_node enables WBS-level material budg']
  actual_received_date date [note: 'Date when the material was received on site.']
  actual_received_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of material actually received on site.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved the MTO line.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the MTO line was approved.']
  compliance_certification varchar [note: 'Applicable certification or standard (e.g., ISO9001, ASTM) that the material meets.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the MTO line record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 code of the currency for cost estimates.']
  design_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the material calculated from design drawings or BIM models.']
  discipline varchar [note: 'Engineering discipline associated with the material.']
  estimated_unit_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated cost per unit of material for procurement planning.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the material is no longer usable or compliant.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material is critical to project schedule or safety.']
  is_deleted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the record has been logically deleted.']
  is_hazardous boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material is classified as hazardous.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Estimated number of days from order to delivery for the material.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the line within the MTO header.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot identifier for traceability of the material.']
  mto_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the MTO line.']
  net_required_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total material quantity required after applying wastage factor.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments or remarks about the MTO line.']
  procurement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the procurement process for this material line.']
  quantity_uom_factor decimal(12,4) [note: 'Factor to convert between alternative units of measure for the material.']
  required_by_date date [note: 'Date by which the material must be on site to meet construction schedule.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Revision identifier of the drawing or BIM model for this line.']
  safety_data_sheet_url varchar [note: 'Link to the safety data sheet document for the material.']
  supplier_preferred varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the preferred supplier for this material.']
  total_estimated_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total estimated cost calculated as net_required_quantity multiplied by estimated_unit_price.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit used for the material quantities.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the MTO line record.']
  variance_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Difference between total_estimated_cost and actual cost incurred.']
  variance_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Difference between net_required_quantity and actual_received_quantity.']
  wastage_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage factor applied to account for material loss during construction.']

  Note: 'Material Take-Off (MTO) line item derived from engineering drawings and BIM models, representing the estimated material quantities required for construction. Captures MTO item, material master reference, design quantity, wastage factor, net required quantity, unit of measure, drawing/BIM model reference, discipline (civil, structural, MEP), revision number, and MTO status (draft, approved, issued-for-procurement). Bridges design domain and procurement/inventory planning.'
}

This is the fundamental project cost control mechanism in construction — requisitions must be WBS-coded f']

  work_front_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.work_front. Business justification: Work-front-driven material demand: site engineers raise material requisitions against a specific work front to ensure materials arrive when and where needed. This link enables work-front-level materia']

  change_order_number varchar [note: 'Associated change order identifier if the requisition results from a scope change.']
  cost_estimate_gross decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected total cost of the requested material before taxes and discounts.']
  cost_estimate_net decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected net cost after tax and any discounts.']
  cost_estimate_tax decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated tax component applicable to the material cost.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter code of the currency used for the cost estimate.']

  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or additional information supplied by the requester.']

  safety_review_status varchar [note: 'Result of the safety compliance review required for certain hazardous materials.']

  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the requested quantity (e.g., kilograms, cubic meters).']

  Note: 'Site or project team request for materials to be issued from warehouse stock or procured. Captures requisition number, requesting WBS/work front, required material, quantity, required-by date, priority, justification, approval status (pending, approved, rejected), approver, and fulfilment status. Triggers either a goods issue (if stock available) or a purchase requisition in procurement. Supports site operations planning and material demand management.'
}

Table material.specification {
  specification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for specification']

Linking material specifications to the governing framework agreement enables compliance a']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to material.material_master. Business justification: Specification defines technical details for a material; linking to material_master removes duplicate type and grade attributes and connects the spec to its parent material.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Required: Material specifications are tied to Regulatory Obligations that define performance and safety standards; essential for compliance verification.']
  rfp_issuance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.rfp_issuance. Business justification: RFP issuances in construction define client-mandated material specifications (grade, standard, compliance). Linking material specifications to the originating RFP enables bid preparation teams to veri']
  skill_trade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.skill_trade. Business justification: Material specifications in construction mandate specific skill trades for installation (e.g., structural steel specs require certified welders, HV cable specs require licensed electricians). This spec']
  technical_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.technical_specification. Business justification: Material specifications must be derived from and comply with the project\'s design technical specifications. The material approval process requires tracing each material specification back to the gover']
  tender_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to bid.tender. Business justification: REQUIRED: Tender documents cite specific material specifications that suppliers must meet; linking enables traceability of spec compliance.']
  applicable_project_type varchar [note: 'Project categories where this specification may be applied.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the specification was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved the specification.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Textual description of regulatory or client compliance requirements tied to the specification.']
  cost_per_unit decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard cost of the material per unit of measure.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the specification record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the cost per unit.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the specification becomes effective for use.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the specification expires or is superseded (nullable).']
