  net_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The final net amount paid or received after all adjustments, discounts, withholding taxes, and bank charges.']
  partial_payment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a partial payment that does not fully settle the referenced invoices or obligations.']
  payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross monetary value of the payment transaction in the transaction currency, before any adjustments or fees.']
  payment_approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for the payment, indicating whether it has been authorized by the appropriate authority levels.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'The interface or system channel through which the payment was initiated or received (e.g., ERP system, banking portal, manual entry).']
  payment_date date [note: 'The actual date when the payment was executed or received. This is the principal business event timestamp for the transaction.']
  payment_document_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique payment document number assigned by the ERP system (SAP S/4HANA or Viewpoint Vista). Used for reconciliation and audit trail.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The financial instrument or mechanism used to execute the payment (e.g., wire transfer, ACH, cheque, cash, LC drawdown).']
  payment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to the payment transaction, capturing special instructions or contextual information.']
  payment_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the payment for processing sequence and cash flow management.']
  payment_purpose varchar [note: 'Business purpose or reason for the payment (e.g., material supply, subcontractor labor, equipment rental, progress billing).']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment transaction in the payment processing workflow.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Contractual payment terms applicable to this transaction (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30), governing due dates and discounts.']
  payment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of payment direction: outgoing payments to vendors/subcontractors (AP disbursements) or incoming receipts from clients (AR collections).']
  po_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order number associated with the payment, typically for vendor payments linked to procurement transactions.']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'Date when the payment was successfully reconciled with the bank statement during the bank reconciliation process.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of bank reconciliation for this payment, indicating whether it has been matched with bank statement entries.']
  remittance_advice varchar [note: 'Detailed remittance information sent to the payee explaining which invoices or obligations are being settled by this payment.']
  retention_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount withheld from the payment as retention per contract terms, to be released upon project completion or defects liability period.']
  value_date date [note: 'The effective date when funds are available or debited in the bank account, used for cash flow and liquidity management.']
  withholding_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax amount withheld at source as per local tax regulations, deducted from the gross payment amount.']

  Note: 'Financial payment transaction record capturing both outgoing payments to vendors/subcontractors (AP disbursements) and incoming receipts from clients (AR collections). Captures payment date, payment method (wire transfer, cheque, ACH, LC drawdown), bank account, amount paid/received, currency, exchange rate, invoice references cleared, bank clearing status, and remittance advice details. Supports multi-currency payment runs, partial payments, and bank reconciliation. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI payment runs and Viewpoint Vista.'
}

Table finance.cash_flow_forecast {
  cash_flow_forecast_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the cash flow forecast record.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Required for cash flow forecasting per contract to align financing with project cash needs; contracts define payment terms used in cash flow models.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project for which this cash flow forecast is prepared. Null for corporate-level forecasts.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cash flow forecasts are prepared per cost center to track cash impact of cost allocations.']
  payment_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.payment_schedule. Business justification: Contractual payment schedules are the primary input to cash flow forecasting. Finance teams build period-level cash flow forecasts from payment schedule installments. Linking forecast entries to speci']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_permit. Business justification: Construction cash flow forecasts must model permit fee payments and renewal cost outflows. Permit expiry and renewal dates drive forecast timing. Finance planners link forecasts to specific permits to']
  phase_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.phase. Business justification: Construction cash flow forecasts are structured by project phase to model phased expenditure profiles (S-curves). Phase-level cash flow forecasting is required for project finance drawdown scheduling,']
  project_milestone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_milestone. Business justification: Cash flow forecasts in construction are milestone-driven: the milestone_payment_amount field on cash_flow_forecast represents scheduled inflows tied to specific contractual milestones. Linking to proj']
  trade_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to bid.trade_package. Business justification: Cash flow forecasts in construction are broken down by trade package for subcontractor payment scheduling. The subcontractor_payment_amount on cash_flow_forecast requires trade package attribution — a']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific WBS element if the forecast is prepared at a granular work package level. Null for project-level or corporate-level forecasts.']
  advance_drawdown_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted drawdown of advance payments or mobilization advances from the client, typically secured by advance payment guarantees.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the forecast was formally approved for use in financial planning and decision-making.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual who approved the forecast, typically the project manager, finance director, or CFO.']
  bond_premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted payments for performance bonds, advance payment guarantees, and other surety instruments required by the contract.']
  bonding_capacity_utilization decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total bonding capacity utilized by this forecast, relevant for corporate-level forecasts to monitor surety capacity constraints.']
  closing_cash_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected cash balance at the end of the forecast period, calculated as opening balance plus net cash flow.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this forecast record was first created in the system.']
  credit_facility_utilization decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of available credit facility or working capital line utilized to meet the peak funding requirement, relevant for corporate treasury management.']
  cumulative_cash_position decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative cash position at the end of the forecast period, representing the running total of net cash flows from project inception or corporate fiscal year start.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this forecast (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  equipment_hire_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted payments for equipment rental and hire charges, including plant, machinery, and temporary facilities.']
  forecast_assumptions varchar [note: 'Key assumptions underlying the forecast, including payment term assumptions, progress rate assumptions, inflation factors, and risk contingencies.']
  forecast_date date [note: 'The date on which this forecast was prepared or last updated, representing the as-of date for the forecast assumptions.']
  forecast_granularity varchar [note: 'The time interval granularity at which cash flows are forecasted: daily for short-term liquidity management, weekly for near-term project cash planning, monthly for standard project forecasting, or quarterly for long-term corporate planning.']
  forecast_identifier varchar [note: 'Business-readable identifier for the forecast, typically combining project code, forecast period, and version (e.g., PRJ-2024-001-V3).']
  forecast_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the period covered by this cash flow forecast.']
  forecast_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the period covered by this cash flow forecast.']
  forecast_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the forecast: draft for work-in-progress, submitted for review, approved for active use, superseded when replaced by a newer version, or archived for historical reference.']
  forecast_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the forecast scope: project-level for individual construction projects, corporate-level for enterprise-wide treasury forecasting, WBS-level for granular work package forecasts, or contract-level for specific contract cash management.']
  forecast_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the forecast, incremented with each revision to track forecast evolution and variance analysis over time.']
  forecasted_inflow_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total projected cash inflows for the forecast period, including client milestone payments, retention releases, advance drawdowns, and other receipts.']
  forecasted_outflow_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total projected cash outflows for the forecast period, including subcontractor payments, material procurement, payroll, equipment hire, bond premiums, and other disbursements.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this forecast record was last updated, supporting audit trail and version control.']
  material_procurement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted payments for material purchases and procurement, based on Purchase Orders (PO) and supplier payment terms.']
  milestone_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted client milestone payments expected during the period, based on contract payment schedules and progress billing milestones.']
  net_cash_flow_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net cash flow for the forecast period, calculated as forecasted inflows minus forecasted outflows, indicating the expected cash position change.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or explanations regarding the forecast, including significant changes from prior versions or special circumstances.']
  opening_cash_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cash balance at the beginning of the forecast period, serving as the starting point for the period\'s cash flow projection.']
  payroll_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted payroll disbursements for direct labor and site personnel, including wages, benefits, and statutory contributions.']
  peak_funding_requirement decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum negative cash position expected during the forecast period, indicating the highest working capital or credit facility draw required.']
  prepared_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual or team who prepared the forecast, typically the project controls team or corporate treasury analyst.']
  retention_release_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted release of retention amounts held by the client, typically released upon project completion or after the Defects Liability Period (DLP).']
  s_curve_profile_indicator varchar [note: 'Classification of the cash flow profile shape: front-loaded for projects with high early expenditure, linear for steady cash flow, back-loaded for projects with late-stage heavy spending, or custom for non-standard profiles.']
  subcontractor_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted payments to subcontractors for work performed, based on subcontract payment terms and progress certificates.']
  variance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage variance between current and prior forecast, calculated as (current - prior) / prior * 100, indicating forecast volatility.']
  variance_to_prior_forecast decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between the current forecast net cash flow and the previous forecast version for the same period, used for forecast accuracy tracking and trend analysis.']
  working_capital_gap decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between forecasted outflows and inflows timing, representing the working capital financing need to bridge payment obligations before receipt of client payments.']

  Note: 'Forward-looking cash flow projection record for construction projects and the corporate entity, capturing forecast inflows (client milestone payments, retention releases, advance drawdowns) and outflows (subcontractor payments, material procurement, payroll, equipment hire, bond premiums) by period (weekly/monthly). Includes S-curve cash flow profile, cumulative cash position, peak funding requirement, working capital gap analysis, and variance to prior forecast. Supports both project-level forecasting for contract cash management and corporate-level treasury forecasting for bonding capacity and credit facility utilization.'
}

Table finance.profit_center {
  profit_center_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for profit_center']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: profit_center does not currently have a company_code_id FK. In SAP S/4HANA and standard construction accounting, profit centers are assigned to company codes for legal entity-level P&L reporting. This']
  parent_profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on profit_center (parent_profit_center_id)']
  actual_profit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual profit realized by the profit center.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the profit center\'s physical address.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned budget allocated to the profit center for the fiscal period.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the profit center\'s primary office.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Associated cost center code used for internal cost allocation.']
  cost_of_goods_sold decimal(18,2) [note: 'Direct costs attributable to the profit center\'s revenue.']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the profit center\'s primary location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the profit center record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  ebit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Earnings before interest and taxes for the profit center.']
  ebitda decimal(18,2) [note: 'Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization for the profit center.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the profit center became effective for reporting.']
  effective_to date [note: 'Date when the profit center ceased to be effective; null if still active.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the profit center in external ERP systems (e.g., SAP, Viewpoint).']
  financial_reporting_standard varchar [note: 'Accounting standard used for the profit center\'s financial reporting.']
  is_consolidated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the profit center\'s results are consolidated into higher‑level reporting.']
  last_reported_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent financial report for the profit center.']
  manager varchar [note: 'Name of the manager responsible for the profit center.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes related to the profit center.']
  owner varchar [note: 'Name of the owner or sponsor of the profit center.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Contact telephone number for the profit center.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal code for the profit center\'s address.']
  profit_center_category varchar [note: 'Business category classification of the profit center.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to identify the profit center in external systems.']
  profit_center_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the profit center\'s purpose and scope.']
  profit_center_level varchar [note: 'Hierarchical level of the profit center within the organization.']
  profit_center_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the profit center.']
  profit_center_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the profit center.']
  profit_center_type varchar [note: 'Category of profit center indicating its organizational role.']
  profit_margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Profit margin expressed as a percentage of revenue.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region where the profit center operates.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the profit center reports financial results.']
  revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total revenue generated by the profit center.']
  tax_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate for the profit center\'s earnings.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the profit center record.']
  wbs_code varchar [note: 'WBS code linking the profit center to project budgeting structures.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for profit_center. Referenced by profit_center_id.'
}

Table finance.company_code {
  company_code_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for company_code']
  parent_company_code_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on company_code (parent_company_code_id)']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the company location.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, floor, etc.).']
  business_area varchar [note: 'Higher‑level business area classification for reporting.']
  chart_of_accounts varchar [note: 'Reference to the chart of accounts used by the entity.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the company is located.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Category describing the legal relationship of the entity within the corporate group.']
  company_code_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric identifier assigned to the legal entity for accounting and reporting.']
  company_code_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the company\'s primary activities and scope.']
  company_code_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the company entity.']
  company_code_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the company entity.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cost center associated with the entity.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the company location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the company code record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code used for reporting for this company.']
  dissolution_date date [note: 'Date the entity was legally dissolved, if applicable.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the company code became effective for accounting purposes.']
  effective_to date [note: 'Date when the company code ceased to be effective (null if still active).']
  external_reference_number varchar [note: 'Identifier used by external partner or regulatory systems to reference this entity.']
  financial_reporting_standard varchar [note: 'Accounting framework applied to the entity\'s financial statements.']
  incorporation_date date [note: 'Date the entity was legally incorporated.']
  industry_code varchar [note: 'Standard industry code (e.g., NAICS) describing the primary business activity.']
  is_consolidated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the entity is included in group financial consolidation.']
  legal_form varchar [note: 'Legal structure of the entity.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code of the company address.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact person.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary person to contact for the company.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary contact person.']
  profit_center varchar [note: 'Identifier of the profit center to which the entity reports.']
  segment varchar [note: 'Business segment to which the entity belongs for strategic reporting.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the company location.']
  tax_number varchar [note: 'Government‑issued tax identifier for the legal entity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the company code record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for company_code. Referenced by company_code_id.'
}

  warranty_period_months varchar [note: 'Duration in months of the defects liability period (DLP) or warranty period after completion.']

  Note: 'Defines a discrete scope-of-work package to be competitively bid and awarded to a subcontractor — e.g., structural steel erection, HVAC installation, electrical fit-out, piling works, or facade cladding. The SSOT for trade package definitions that drive the subcontractor bidding and award process.'
}

Table bid.boq {
  boq_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each BOQ master record.']
  bim_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.bim_model. Business justification: Required for Quantity Takeoff: BOQ creation uses BIM model geometry to derive quantities; the BOQ must reference the BIM model used.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign‑key hint referencing the overarching construction project for which the BOQ is prepared.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Links BOQ preparation to a finance cost center, enabling detailed cost center reporting for bill of quantities and supporting budget approvals.']
  drawing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.drawing. Business justification: Required for Quantity Takeoff: BOQ lines are derived from construction drawings; linking each BOQ to its source drawing enables traceability.']
  estimate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to bid.estimate. Business justification: A Bill of Quantities (BOQ) is the structured pricing document attached to a tender, while an estimate is the internal cost build-up. In construction bid practice, the BOQ is typically prepared from or']
  technical_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.technical_specification. Business justification: BOQ preparation references technical specifications for measurement rules, material standards, and CSI division alignment. Estimators link each BOQ to the governing spec. \'specification_standard\' is a']
  tender_id varchar [note: 'Foreign‑key hint linking the BOQ to the tender (project bid) it supports.']
  wbs_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to schedule.wbs_node. Business justification: BOQ sections are mapped to WBS nodes for cost-schedule integration and earned value management — quantity surveyors structure the BOQ against the project WBS to enable BCWP/BCWS calculations. boq has ']
  approval_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the BOQ was signed off by the project controls authority.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual who granted final approval to the BOQ.']
  boq_description varchar [note: 'Narrative field for additional context, special instructions, or remarks from the estimator.']
  boq_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the BOQ is still being edited, has been issued to bidders, approved, revised, or archived.']
  boq_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the BOQ as measured (based on take‑off), provisional (estimated), or approximate (high‑level).']
  contains_confidential_pricing boolean [note: 'True if the BOQ includes pricing that must be treated as confidential per contract and regulatory policy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time the BOQ master record entered the data lake.']
  currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter currency identifier (e.g., USD, EUR) used for the total_value field.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Rate used to convert the BOQ total_value to the company\'s reporting currency.']
  exchange_rate_date date [note: 'Effective date for the exchange_rate value.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Optional end‑date after which the BOQ cannot be used for pricing; null if open‑ended.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the BOQ was released to bidders as part of the tender package.']
  preparation_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the BOQ document was initially compiled.']
  reference varchar [note: 'Human‑readable code used to identify the BOQ within the tender package.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Incremental integer indicating the version of the BOQ; higher numbers represent later revisions.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Indicates which operational system of record supplied the BOQ data.']
  total_quantity decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate of the quantity field from all BOQ line items, expressed in the unit defined by the specification standard.']
  total_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate price of all line items in the BOQ, expressed in the selected currency.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Login or employee identifier of the last person to modify the BOQ.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the latest modification to any BOQ field.']
  version_label varchar [note: 'Human‑readable version identifier shown on the BOQ cover page.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Login or employee identifier of the estimator who initially created the BOQ.']

  Note: 'Bill of Quantities master record defining the structured pricing document attached to a tender. Captures BOQ reference, revision number, BOQ type (measured, provisional, approximate), total BOQ value, currency, preparation date, and the specification standard applied (NRM, POMI, CESMM). Each BOQ is linked to a tender and decomposed into BOQ line items. SSOT for BOQ document identity and header-level pricing data.'
}

Table bid.bid_boq_line {
  bid_boq_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the BOQ line item.']
  asset_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.asset_category. Business justification: BOQ plant cost quantification: quantity surveyors classify plant cost BOQ lines by equipment category to apply standard hire rates and benchmark against market rates. Linking bid_boq_line to asset_cat']
  boq_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent BOQ header.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: BOQ lines are allocated to cost centers for budget control and cost reporting. The plain `cost_center_code` on bid_boq_line is a denormalization of finance.cost_center. Construction cost controllers r']
  cost_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_code. Business justification: BOQ lines must be classified against cost codes for budget commitment and cost control reporting. In construction, every BOQ line item maps to a cost code to enable bid-to-budget reconciliation and ea']
  labor_cost_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.labor_cost_code. Business justification: BOQ line items include a labour_cost component requiring a labor cost code for burden rate application, prevailing wage compliance, and cost code alignment between bid BOQ and project cost control. St']
  technical_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.technical_specification. Business justification: Each BOQ line item is priced against a specification section (CSI division/work section). Named process: \'specification-referenced BOQ line pricing\'. \'work_section\' is a denormalized text field replac']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.wbs_element. Business justification: During project execution, BOQ lines are mapped to WBS elements for cost control and EVM. Construction cost engineers routinely reconcile BOQ quantities against WBS actuals for payment certification an']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the work was actually completed.']
  bid_boq_line_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the work item.']
  bid_boq_line_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the BOQ line.']
  change_order_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the line originated from a change order.']
  change_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the associated change order, if any.']
  cost_category varchar [note: 'Classification of cost type for budgeting and reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the line record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary values.']
  estimated_completion_date date [note: 'Planned date for completing the work represented by the line.']
  is_critical_path boolean [note: 'Marks the line as part of the project critical path.']
  is_gmp_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates if the line is included in a Guaranteed Maximum Price bid.']
  is_lump_sum boolean [note: 'Indicates if the line is part of a lump‑sum bid component.']
  is_taxable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the line item is subject to tax.']
  item_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code for the work item as defined in the company\'s catalog.']
  labour_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cost component attributable to labour for the line item.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the line within the BOQ.']
  material_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cost component attributable to materials.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for additional remarks or clarifications.']
  overhead_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Allocated overhead amount for the line item.']
  plant_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cost component attributable to plant/equipment usage.']
  profit_margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target profit margin percentage applied to the line.']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the item required as per take‑off.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the line item after changes.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification for the line item.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record from which the line originated (e.g., Procore, SAP).']
  subcontract_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cost component attributable to subcontracted work.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Calculated tax amount for the line item.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate percentage for taxable items.']
  total_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Calculated total cost for the line (quantity × unit_rate).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the quantity (e.g., meter, kilogram, piece). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: unit|kg|m|m2|m3|l|pcs|hr — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unit_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'Price per unit of measure for the item.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the line record.']

  Note: 'Individual line item within a Bill of Quantities, representing a discrete work item priced during bid preparation. Captures item code, WBS reference, work section, item description, unit of measure, quantity, unit rate, total amount, labour component, plant component, material component, subcontract component, and overhead allocation. Supports MTO-driven quantity take-off and rate build-up analysis. Essential for bid-to-actual cost variance tracking post-award.'
}

Table bid.estimate_line {
  estimate_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the estimate line item.']
  asset_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.asset_category. Business justification: Category-level equipment estimating: estimators price plant costs by equipment category (e.g., \'crawler crane 100T\') before specific assets are assigned. estimate_line already has asset_id for specifi']

The plain `cost_center_code` on estimate_line is a denormalization of finance.cost_center. Construction estimators as']
  cost_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_code. Business justification: Estimate lines are classified by cost code for estimate-vs-actual variance reporting — a core construction cost control process. The plain `cost_code` string on estimate_line is a denormalization of f']
  estimate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent estimate (transaction header) to which this line belongs.']

This is standard construction estimating practice —']
  labor_rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.labor_rate. Business justification: Each estimate line may apply a specific labor rate; linking to workforce.labor_rate enables precise labor cost per line in bid estimates.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to material.material_master. Business justification: REQUIRED: Estimating uses master material data for unit pricing, compliance and spec reference, essential for accurate bid cost calculations.']
  resource_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the resource (item, equipment, labour) associated with this line.']
  skill_trade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.skill_trade. Business justification: Labor estimate lines are built by trade (ironworker, electrician, pipefitter). Linking to skill_trade enables productivity factor lookup, standard crew size, and prevailing wage classification during ']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.wbs_element. Business justification: Estimate lines are mapped to WBS elements to build the project cost structure during estimating and for post-award cost baseline reconciliation. Construction estimators and cost controllers depend on ']
  approval_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the line was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who approved the line.']
  baseline_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Original cost captured at baseline creation.']

  cost_variance decimal(18,4) [note: 'Difference between revised and baseline cost.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the estimate line was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary values.']
  estimate_line_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the estimate line.']
  estimate_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the estimate containing this line.']
  is_deleted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the line has been soft‑deleted.']
  labor_rate_type varchar [note: 'Basis for labour cost calculation.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the line within the estimate.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the site or geographic location for the line.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Classification of material used for the line.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the line.']
  productivity_factor decimal(5,3) [note: 'Multiplier applied to account for expected productivity (e.g., 1.05).']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Amount of the resource required.']
  resource_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the resource.']
  retention_status varchar [note: 'Retention state of the line for contractual hold‑backs.']
  revised_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cost after revisions or updates.']
  risk_factor decimal(5,3) [note: 'Multiplier reflecting risk or contingency applied to the line.']
  source_of_rate decimal(18,2) [note: 'Origin of the unit cost used for this line.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary tax amount calculated for the line.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate percentage for the line.']
  total_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Calculated total cost (quantity × unit cost) before adjustments.']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cost per unit of the resource in the selected currency.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit used for the quantity (e.g., meters, kilograms).']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the line.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the line.']
  variance_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for the cost variance.']
  waste_factor decimal(5,3) [note: 'Multiplier to include anticipated material waste.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the line.']

  Note: 'Detailed cost line item within a project estimate, capturing the granular cost build-up for a specific work package or activity. Stores cost code, WBS element, cost category (labour, material, plant, subcontract, indirect), resource description, quantity, unit, unit cost, total cost, productivity factor, waste factor, and source of rate (historical, vendor quote, published index). Enables detailed cost-to-complete analysis and estimate benchmarking.'
}

Required']

  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center.   rfp_issuance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.rfp_issuance. Business justification: A submission is the contractor\'s formal response to a specific RFP issuance. Linking submission to rfp_issuance enables submission deadline compliance tracking, bid/no-bid analysis per RFP, and addend']
  tender_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to bid.tender. Business justification: A submission is the transactional record of the formal act of submitting a tender to a client. It belongs to exactly one tender. One tender can have multiple submissions (e.g., initial submission, rev']
  acknowledgement_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number received from the client confirming receipt.']
  bid_bond_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the bid bond required for this submission.']

  bid_bond_type varchar [note: 'Type of bid bond provided.']
  bid_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary amount offered in the bid at submission.']
  bid_price_adjustment decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any adjustment (e.g., discount, fee) applied to the base bid price.']

  evaluation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to evaluate the bid (e.g., two‑envelope).']

  submission_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the bid submission.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the formal act of submitting a tender to a client or owner. Records submission timestamp, submission method (electronic portal, hard copy, email), submitted by (person), submission reference number, number of copies submitted, bid price at submission, technical score (if two-envelope), commercial score, submission acknowledgement reference, and late submission flag. Represents the definitive bid submission event for audit and compliance purposes.'
}

  cost_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_code. Business justification: Clarifications with financial impact (impact_amount, impact_flag_price) must be classified by cost code to update the estimate and BOQ. Construction estimators reclassify clarification impacts against']

Business justification: Tender clarifications frequently concern permit status — bidders ask whether environmental or planning permits are secured before pricing. Linking clarification to compliance_permit allows bid teams t']

  technical_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.technical_specification. Business justification: Tender clarifications frequently challenge or seek interpretation of specific specification sections. Named business process: \'specification clarification during tender\'. Construction procurement team']
  tender_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to bid.tender. Business justification: Clarifications are formal tender-period communications (RFIs, addenda, scope clarifications) that occur within the context of a specific tender. A clarification belongs to one tender; one tender can h']
  acknowledgement_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the recipient has formally acknowledged receipt.']
  addendum_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the addendum referenced, if the communication is an addendum or amendment.']
  attachment_flag boolean [note: 'True if supporting files are attached to the clarification.']
  bid_revision_triggered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the clarification caused a formal bid revision.']
  clarification_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the clarification by the tendering authority.']

  communication_type varchar [note: 'Category of the communication exchanged during the tender period.']
  content varchar [note: 'Full text of the original communication request or notice.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the clarification record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the impact amount.']
  direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the communication originated from the client or the contractor.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the communication was originally issued.']
  impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary impact of the clarification on the bid.']

  impact_flag_scope boolean [note: 'True if the clarification modifies the project scope.']
  impact_flag_specifications boolean [note: 'True if the clarification changes technical specifications or drawings.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Marks the clarification as critical to bid success.']
  issuing_authority varchar [note: 'Entity that issued the communication (e.g., client, architect, engineer).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments added by users.']
  response_content varchar [note: 'Full text of the response provided to the communication.']
  response_deadline date [note: 'Date by which the recipient must respond to the communication.']
  response_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the response process.']
  response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the response was received or recorded.']
  revised_submission_deadline date [note: 'New deadline for bid submission if the clarification triggers a deadline change.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision count for the clarification record.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating source system (e.g., Procore, Aconex).']
  subject varchar [note: 'Brief summary of the communication subject.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the clarification record.']

  Note: 'Record of all formal tender-period communications and document modifications including RFIs (Requests for Information), clarifications, client-issued addenda, and amendments that modify the RFP/RFQ documents. Captures communication reference, type (RFI_issued, RFI_received, addendum, amendment, clarification), direction, addendum number (where applicable), issuing authority, subject, content, response, impact flags (bid price, schedule, scope, BOQ, specifications, drawings), revised submission deadline (if applicable), acknowledgement status, bid revision triggered, and status (open, answered, closed, incorporated). SSOT for all tender-period correspondence, addenda tracking, and document amendment audit trail ensuring tender compliance.'
}

Table bid.bond {
  bond_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the bid bond record.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the construction project associated with the bid.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Maps bid bond liability to a GL account for proper accounting entry, essential for financial statements and bond guarantee reporting.']
  tender_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the tender to which this bid bond is attached.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the bid bond.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved the bid bond.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bid bond was formally approved by the authorized authority.']
  beneficiary varchar [note: 'Client or project owner that is the beneficiary of the bid bond.']
  bond_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the bid bond by the issuing entity.']
  bond_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the bid bond.']
  bond_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the bond instrument (e.g., bank guarantee, surety bond, insurance bond).']
  compliance_requirements_met boolean [note: 'True if the bond satisfies all regulatory and client‑specific compliance requirements.']
  confidentiality_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the bond details are marked as confidential for internal handling.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bid bond record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency in which the bond amount is expressed.']
  documents_attached boolean [note: 'Indicates whether supporting bond documents have been attached to the record.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date the bid bond expires or must be returned.']
  expiry_place varchar [note: 'Geographic location where the bond is to be returned or expires.']
  extension_count varchar [note: 'Total count of times the bond expiry has been extended.']
  guarantee_extension_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the bond terms permit extensions.']
  guarantee_extension_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for the most recent bond extension.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the bid bond was formally issued.']
  issue_place varchar [note: 'Geographic location (city/country) where the bond was issued.']
  issuer_type varchar [note: 'Category of the issuing entity: bank, surety, or insurance.']
  issuing_entity varchar [note: 'Name of the bank, surety, or insurance company that issued the bid bond.']
  last_extension_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent expiry extension.']
  last_updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who performed the most recent update to the record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the bid bond.']
  percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Bond amount expressed as a percentage of the total tender value.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk assessment rating assigned to the bond based on financial and contractual factors.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source operational system that supplied the bond data (e.g., SAP, Procore).']
  status_date date [note: 'Date on which the current status became effective.']
  total_extension_days varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of days added to the original expiry date through extensions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the bid bond record.']

  Note: 'Master record for a bid bond (tender guarantee) instrument required as part of a tender submission. Captures bond reference number, issuing bank or surety, bond amount, bond currency, bond percentage of tender value, issue date, expiry date, bond type (bank guarantee, surety bond, insurance bond), beneficiary (client), bond status (issued, submitted, returned, forfeited, extended), and extension history. Tracks bond lifecycle from issuance through return or forfeiture.'
}

Table bid.risk {
  risk_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the bid risk record.']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.construction_project. Business justification: Risk register – risks identified in a bid are tracked against the associated construction project for integrated risk management.']
  cost_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_code. Business justification: Bid risks with contingency_amount must be classified by cost code for contingency budget allocation and risk-adjusted cost reporting. Construction risk managers assign cost codes to quantified risks t']
  permit_condition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit_condition. Business justification: Risk register links each risk to a specific permit condition that may trigger compliance penalties.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Bid risks in construction are frequently driven by regulatory obligations — new environmental regulations, safety mandates, or local content requirements create cost and schedule risk. Linking bid_ris']
  tender_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the tender associated with the risk.']
  assessment_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to evaluate the risk.']
  attached_documents varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of document identifiers linked to the risk.']
  bid_risk_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the risk.']
  closed_date date [note: 'Date the risk was formally closed.']
  contingency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Financial reserve allocated to cover the risk in the bid price.']
  contingency_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contingency expressed as a percentage of the estimated cost impact.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the risk record was created in the system.']
  exposure_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary impact fields.']
  identified_date date [note: 'Date the risk was first recorded.']
  impact_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary impact if the risk materialises, expressed in the project currency.']
  impact_quality varchar [note: 'Qualitative effect of the risk on project quality standards.']
  impact_schedule_days varchar [note: 'Estimated schedule delay in days caused by the risk.']
  is_key_risk boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the risk is considered a key risk for the bid.']
  mitigation_end_date date [note: 'Planned or actual completion date for mitigation.']
  mitigation_start_date date [note: 'Planned start date for mitigation activities.']
  mitigation_strategy varchar [note: 'Planned actions to reduce probability or impact of the risk.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments added by risk owners.']
  origin varchar [note: 'Origin of the risk, indicating whether it arises from internal processes or external factors.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Prioritisation level used for reporting and escalation.']
  probability_rating varchar [note: 'Likelihood of the risk occurring, expressed as a qualitative rating.']
  residual_impact_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary impact remaining after mitigation.']
  residual_probability varchar [note: 'Likelihood of the risk after mitigation actions are applied.']
  residual_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk score after mitigation, used for ongoing monitoring.']
  review_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the risk is reviewed and re‑assessed.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the risk affecting the bid phase.']
  risk_code varchar [note: 'External reference code used by project teams to identify the risk in reports and documents.']
  risk_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the nature, cause and potential consequences of the risk.']
  score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite score derived from probability and impact, used for prioritisation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that supplied the risk data (e.g., Procore, SAP).']
  title varchar [note: 'Brief human‑readable title that summarises the risk.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the risk record.']

  Note: 'Risk register entry specific to the bid phase, capturing identified risks and opportunities that influence bid pricing strategy and contingency allocation. Stores risk ID, risk category (commercial, technical, geotechnical, regulatory, supply chain, force majeure), risk description, probability rating, impact rating (cost, schedule, quality), risk score, mitigation strategy, contingency amount allocated, risk owner, and residual risk rating. Feeds bid pricing decisions and GMP contingency calculations.'
}

Table bid.approval {
  approval_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the bid approval record.']
  assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_assessment. Business justification: Bid approval decisions incorporate results of a compliance assessment report to ensure regulatory fit.']
  bid_opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to bid.bid_opportunity. Business justification: Bid approval should be linked to the originating bid opportunity for traceability.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Bid approvals (go/no-go) are governed by delegation of authority frameworks defined at company code level. The delegation_of_authority_level on approval maps to company code governance structures — a ']
  estimate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to bid.estimate. Business justification: An approval event governs a specific bid price and margin (approved_bid_price, approved_margin_pct attributes on approval). These values are derived from and validated against a specific estimate. Lin']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Bid go/no-go approvals are governed by delegation of authority at the profit center level. The decision_authority_role and approved_margin_pct on approval are evaluated against profit center targets —']
  tender_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to bid.tender. Business justification: An approval is a formal bid governance decision event. While approval already links to bid_opportunity (the pipeline-level record), a direct link to the specific tender being approved is essential for']
  approval_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable reference number assigned to the approval event per internal governance policy.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the approval record.']
  approved_bid_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final approved monetary amount for the bid.']
  approved_margin_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Approved profit margin expressed as a percentage of the bid price.']
  bonding_capacity_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score reflecting the company\'s ability to provide required bid bonds.']
  client_relationship_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score reflecting the strength of the relationship with the client.']
  conditions_imposed varchar [note: 'Any contractual or operational conditions attached to the approval.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bid approval record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the approved bid price.']
  deadline date [note: 'Date by which the approved bid must be submitted.']
  decision_authority_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the person or entity authorizing the decision.']
  decision_authority_role varchar [note: 'Organizational role or title of the decision authority.']
  decision_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the approval process (e.g., bid, no_bid, conditional_bid, approved, approved_with_conditions, rejected, deferred). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bid|no_bid|conditional_bid|approved|approved_with_conditions|rejected|deferred — promote to reference product]']
  decision_rationale varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation of the reasoning behind the decision.']
  decision_stage varchar [note: 'Governance stage at which the decision was taken.']
  decision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the approval decision was recorded.']
  delegation_of_authority_level varchar [note: 'Authorized delegation tier that permitted the decision.']
  is_conditional boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the approval is subject to conditions.']
  margin_potential_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected profitability score based on estimated margins.']
  pursuit_investment_justification varchar [note: 'Business case narrative justifying the investment of resources into the bid pursuit.']
  resource_availability_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score indicating the availability of required resources for the project.']
  risk_profile_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score assessing the risk exposure of the bid.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk classification assigned to the bid pursuit.']
  strategic_fit_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score measuring alignment of the bid with corporate strategy.']
  total_governance_score decimal(10,2) [note: 'Aggregated score from all governance criteria used to evaluate the bid.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the bid approval record.']
  valid_until date [note: 'Expiration date of the approval if not acted upon.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a formal bid governance decision event within the internal authorization workflow, capturing the complete pursuit lifecycle from initial bid/no-bid decision through estimating review, commercial review, risk review, and executive sign-off. Stores approval stage (bid_no_bid, estimate_review, commercial_review, risk_review, executive_signoff), decision date, decision outcome (bid, no-bid, conditional_bid, approved, approved_with_conditions, rejected, deferred), decision authority (approver name and role), scoring against governance criteria (client relationship, risk profile, resource availability, margin potential, strategic fit, bonding capacity), total governance score, risk rating, approved bid price, approved margin percentage, conditions imposed, decision rationale narrative, delegation of authority level, and pursuit investment justification. SSOT for all bid governance decisions including pursuit authorization, resource allocation, and complete audit trail from opportunity pursuit through final submission authority.'
}

Table compliance.permit {

Business justification: A construction permit is frequently issued based on or conditional upon an approved Environmental Impact Assessment. Linking compliance_permit to env_impact_assessment formalizes this dependency, enab']
  regulatory_authority_id varchar [note: 'FK to compliance.regulatory_authority']

  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the permit becomes effective (may differ from issue date).']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date until which the permit remains in effect (nullable for open-ended).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date the permit expires or must be renewed.']
  fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary fee associated with the permit.']
  fee_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the fee.']
  fee_paid_date date [note: 'Date the permit fee was paid.']
  fee_paid_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the permit fee has been paid.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates if the permit is currently active (not expired, revoked, or suspended).']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the permit was officially issued.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the permit record.']
  next_condition_due_date date [note: 'Earliest due date among pending permit conditions.']

  permit_category varchar [note: 'Category of the permit based on ownership or project type.']
  permit_number varchar [note: 'Official permit number assigned by issuing authority.']

  suspension_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the permit is currently suspended.']
  suspension_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for permit suspension.']

  Note: 'Master register of all construction permits across the full lifecycle from application through expiry, including building permits, excavation permits, environmental permits, utility connection permits, demolition permits, and occupancy permits. Tracks application submissions (applicant details, supporting documents, authority response, resubmissions, appeals), authority review and approval/rejection, issuance, individual conditions attached (each with due date, responsible party, fulfilment status, and compliance evidence), condition compliance monitoring, renewal, expiry, revocation, and suspension. Captures permit number, issuing authority, permit type, project association, submission date, issue date, expiry date, renewal requirements, and current status. Serves as the SSOT for permit identity, permit application history, and permit condition compliance across all project sites.'
}

  Note: 'Master record for Environmental Impact Assessments (EIA), Environmental Management Plans (EMP), and ongoing environmental monitoring programs for construction projects. Captures assessment type, scope, environmental consultant, assessment date, findings summary, mitigation measures required, regulatory submission reference, approval status, and review cycle. Includes full environmental monitoring history: air quality measurements, noise level readings, water discharge sampling, soil contamination checks, dust suppression monitoring, with monitoring date, location, parameter measured, recorded value, regulatory threshold, exceedance flag, and corrective action triggered for each monitoring event. Supports EPA reporting, ISO 14001 surveillance, and demonstrates ongoing environmental compliance through continuous monitoring evidence.'
}

  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version for optimistic concurrency control.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of formal regulatory submissions made to governing bodies including OSHA, EPA, local building authorities, and environmental agencies. Captures submission type (annual report, incident notification, permit renewal, compliance certificate), submission date, regulatory body, submission reference number, reporting period, submission method, acknowledgement received, and submission status. Serves as the SSOT for all outbound regulatory filings.'
}

  Note: 'Master register of all regulatory obligations applicable to the organization and its construction projects, derived from applicable laws, standards, permits, and contractual requirements. Captures obligation source (OSHA 29 CFR, EPA Clean Water Act, IBC/IRC, ISO 45001, GDPR, PCI DSS, FIDIC, local building codes), obligation description, applicable jurisdiction, effective date, review frequency, responsible owner, compliance evidence required, current compliance status, and organizational scope mapping (projects, business units, jurisdictions). Serves as the single compliance obligation inventory underpinning all compliance activities, assessments, calendar deadlines, and regulatory change impact analysis.'
}

  action_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the compliance action record.']

  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Compliance actions incur costs that must be posted to a GL account for financial reporting; linking ensures accurate expense accounting.']
  ncr_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.ncr. Business justification: NCRs with regulatory implications (structural failures, environmental breaches) directly trigger compliance actions. Compliance managers need compliance_action-to-NCR linkage to track which quality no']

  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Compliance actions address regulatory obligations; many actions can belong to one obligation. Adding regulatory_obligation_id to compliance_action enables this relationship.']

  action_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier assigned to the corrective or preventive action, often used in reports and communications.']
  action_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the action as corrective, preventive, or improvement.']

  cost_estimate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected monetary cost to implement the action.']

  due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the action must be completed to satisfy the compliance requirement.']

  is_repeat_action boolean [note: 'True if this action is a repeat of a previously recorded issue.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the action record was last reviewed for relevance or accuracy.']
  mitigation_plan varchar [note: 'Long‑term plan to prevent recurrence of the issue.']

  Note: 'Corrective and preventive actions raised as a result of compliance assessments, regulatory findings, permit condition breaches, or authority notices. Tracks action description, root cause, responsible party, due date, priority, completion status, evidence of closure, and verification date. Distinct from safety corrective actions (owned by safety domain via Intelex) — this covers regulatory compliance remediation only.'
}

Table compliance.compliance_calendar {
  compliance_calendar_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the compliance calendar entry.']
  assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.assessment. Business justification: The compliance_calendar tracks assessment due dates and next assessment dates. Linking calendar entries to assessment records enables tracking of scheduled vs. completed assessments. The FK is nullabl']
  authority_notice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.authority_notice. Business justification: The compliance_calendar tracks response deadlines and remediation deadlines for authority notices. Linking calendar entries to authority_notice records enables deadline management for notice responses']
  construction_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.construction_project. Business justification: Compliance calendar deadlines (permit renewals, regulatory reporting deadlines, condition due dates) are project-specific. Project-level compliance deadline dashboards and PMO compliance status report']
  parent_compliance_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on compliance_calendar (parent_compliance_calendar_id)']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: The compliance_calendar tracks obligation review dates, reporting deadlines, and compliance milestones. Linking calendar entries to specific regulatory_obligation records enables obligation-level dead']
  regulatory_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_submission. Business justification: The compliance_calendar tracks submission due dates. Linking calendar entries to regulatory_submission records enables tracking of whether a scheduled submission has been completed. The FK is nullable']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.site. Business justification: Compliance calendar deadlines (monthly reporting, permit renewals, inspection dates) are site-specific obligations. Site managers need a site-filtered compliance calendar view — a standard constructio']
  attachment_count varchar [note: 'Number of supporting documents attached to the calendar entry.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the compliance action was completed.']
  compliance_calendar_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the calendar entry.']
  compliance_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of compliance (e.g., Safety, Environment, Quality).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the calendar entry record was first created.']
  deadline_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the compliance obligation must be satisfied.']
  deadline_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the deadline, indicating the nature of the compliance requirement.']
  entry_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the calendar entry, used for external reference.']
  escalation_path varchar [note: 'Textual description of the escalation procedure if the deadline is missed.']
  escalation_triggered_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the escalation process has been activated.']
  escalation_triggered_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the escalation was triggered.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code indicating the jurisdiction governing the obligation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or additional information about the calendar entry.']
  overdue_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the deadline is past due and not yet completed.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the deadline for resource planning.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory agency or body that issued the obligation.']
  reminder_lead_days varchar [note: 'Number of days prior to the deadline when a reminder should be issued.']
  reminder_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the pre‑deadline reminder has been sent.']
  reminder_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reminder was sent to the responsible owner.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk rating associated with missing the deadline.']
  source_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code or identifier of the originating regulatory obligation, permit, or certification.']
  title varchar [note: 'Human‑readable title describing the compliance deadline or event.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the calendar entry.']

  Note: 'Operational calendar of compliance deadlines, renewal dates, submission due dates, permit expiry dates, ISO audit schedules, LEED submission windows, and regulatory filing deadlines. Aggregates key dates from permits, certifications, regulatory obligations, and authority notices into a single actionable timeline. Captures deadline date, deadline type, source obligation or permit reference, responsible owner, reminder lead time, escalation path, completion status, and overdue flag. The primary daily operational tool for construction compliance managers ensuring no regulatory deadline is missed across multiple project sites and jurisdictions.'
}

Table compliance.obligation_assessment_result {
  obligation_assessment_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the obligation_assessment_result association']
  assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the parent compliance assessment in which this obligation was evaluated.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the specific regulatory obligation evaluated in this assessment result.']
  compliance_rating varchar [note: 'Compliance result for this specific obligation within this assessment. Distinct from the aggregate compliance_rating on the assessment record, which rolls up all obligations.']
  evaluated_date date [note: 'Date on which this specific obligation was evaluated within the assessment. May differ from the overall assessment_date when assessments span multiple days or phases. Sourced from detection phase (obligation_assessment_date).']
  findings varchar [note: 'Specific audit findings or observations recorded for this obligation during this assessment. Belongs to the intersection, not to the assessment summary or the obligation master record.']
  gap_status varchar [note: 'Whether a compliance gap was identified for this specific obligation in this assessment. Sourced directly from detection phase relationship data.']
  obligations_assessed varchar [note: 'List or description of regulatory obligations evaluated during the assessment. [Moved from assessment: This STRING field is a denormalized, multi-value representation of the M:N relationship between assessment and regulatory_obligation. It must be removed and replaced by the obligation_assessment_result association table, which properly normalizes each obligation-assessment intersection as a discrete record.]']
  remediation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether remediation action is required for this obligation as a result of this assessment finding. Sourced directly from detection phase relationship data (remediation_required_flag).']

  Note: 'This association product represents the per-obligation outcome Event between assessment and regulatory_obligation. It captures the specific compliance result, findings, gap status, and remediation requirement for each regulatory obligation evaluated within a given compliance assessment. Each record links one assessment to one regulatory_obligation with attributes that exist only in the context of this intersection — a named operational concept used in ISO 14001, OSHA VPP, and EPA audit frameworks.. Existence Justification: In construction compliance management, a single assessment (e.g., an EPA audit or OSHA inspection) evaluates multiple regulatory obligations simultaneously, and each regulatory obligation is evaluated across multiple assessments over its lifecycle. This is not an analytical correlation — compliance teams actively create, manage, and report on per-obligation findings within each assessment as a named operational concept (\'obligation assessment result\'). The relationship carries its own data (per-obligation compliance rating, findings, gap status, remediation flag) that belongs to neither the assessment nor the obligation alone.'
}
